value.Title: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHF-8HY3-S51B-P182-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP BACKS PURE SALMON JAPAN WITH USD 180 MILLION PREFERRED-NOTE FINANCING

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is designed to support the construction of the land-based facility and to help meet growing domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores Fortress Investment Group’s longstanding presence in Japan and its willingness to provide both capital and strategic guidance to Pure Salmon Japan. The closing signals strong institutional backing for the project and aligns with Fortress’s broader focus on sustainable infrastructure.The project targets a production capacity of 10,000 tonnes and aims to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will use advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to minimize imports and bolster food security.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress officials also noted the value of the partnership; Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, called the investment a meaningful step in strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure. As construction progresses, Pure Salmon Japan intends to pursue additional long-term equity partners to enhance operations.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST DEAL, AGEWELL TO OPERATE AS PART OF GROWTH IN SENIOR LIVING PORTFOLIO

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida. The transaction, funded by Fortress affiliates, was completed on 12th March 2026, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care.Established in 2018, the community sits on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, serving an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast.Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved significantly, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.Peter Stone, Head of Healthcare Real Estate Equity at Fortress, said: "We are pleased to expand our seniors housing portfolio through the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast. This is a high-quality asset in a supply-constrained, demographically attractive market. We look forward to working with AgeWell on the ongoing execution of the plan to deliver exceptional care and hospitality to residents and families."The acquisition underscores Fortress Investment Group's continued commitment to investing in well-located, high-quality senior housing assets that are believed to possess significant long-term growth potential. Founded in 1998, Fortress Investment Group manages $54 billion of assets on behalf of approximately 2,000 institutional clients and private investors worldwide across various investment strategies.

+ FORTRESS PROVIDES WHOLE-LOAN FINANCING FOR CITYVIEW'S 296-UNIT JASPER NEAR USC

Fortress Investment Group has closed a $96 million whole loan to Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily project in Downtown Los Angeles. The transaction, completed on Feb. 9, 2026, was arranged by Fortress affiliates as part of the firm's ongoing support for large-scale real estate financings.Jasper, owned and developed by Cityview, is located near the University of Southern California. The property offers studio, one- and two-bedroom units with high-end finishes and a full suite of amenities, including affordable housing units. The loan proceeds will refinance construction debt and facilitate leasing efforts for the newly opened community.Cityview's Damian Gancman highlighted Fortress's efficient execution and collaborative approach throughout the deal. At Fortress, Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt, noted that Jasper reflects Fortress's focus on backing high-quality, newly built multifamily assets backed by experienced sponsors with meaningful equity. Fortress Investment Group, founded in 1998, is a diversified global investment manager with about $54 billion in assets under management as of Sept. 30, 2025, pursuing credit and real estate, private equity and permanent capital investments.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ VERTIS INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST (NYSE:VIT) TARGETS UP TO ₹3,000 CRORE VIA IPO AND SECONDARY SALE

Vertis Infrastructure Trust (NYSE:VIT) plans to file a draft red herring prospectus by end May 2026 to raise up to ₹3,000 crore through a combination of primary and secondary share sales. The InvIT portfolio centers on toll and annuity road projects across India, built largely through acquisitions by KKR's Asian infrastructure fund which launched the platform in 2021 and expanded in 2024-25 with the acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The trust oversees 28 road projects across nine states, valued at ₹26,500 crore.Insiders note that the final offer size could be trimmed as the trust has limited requirements for new primary capital. The pricing and scale will reflect this cautious assessment within a competitive InvIT landscape. Investors have shown continued interest in InvITs as a way to access long-duration, infrastructure-backed yields.Recent sector activity underscores the momentum in InvIT fundraising, with NHAI raising over ₹9,000 crore through two InvIT offerings in March and Citius Transnet Investment Trust raising ₹1,105 crore in April, reinforcing demand despite market volatility. Vertis Infrastructure Trust remains focused on executing a disciplined and scalable IPO strategy

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) SETS $10 BILLION BUDGET FOR AI INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) will invest $10 billion toward AI-focused power plants and data centers, a move that expands capital dedicated to AI infrastructure in the United States.On 3rd May 2026, the commitment sits within a broader industry effort, with Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta collectively directing more than $100 billion worldwide into similar projects.The investment is intended to secure long-term power agreements, giving KKR leverage in power generation as demand from AI data centers climbs.The plan also intersects geopolitics, as Canada and Germany consolidate activity to counterbalance U.S.–China AI influence; KKR's participation could influence this evolving landscape.Market signals connect the investment to Google's AI development trajectory, and analysts weigh the potential for Google's stock to rise toward $310 as the infrastructure builds out.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE: KKR) SETS SPOTLIGHT AT BANK OF AMERICA SECURITIES CONFERENCE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) will have its Chief Financial Officer Robert H. Lewin present at the Bank of America Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on Feb. 10, 2026 at 2:40 PM ET. The presentation will be delivered as part of the conference program. The event will be accessible through a live webcast in KKR's Investor Center, enabling real-time participation by investors worldwide, with a replay posted shortly after the session. KKR is a global investment firm that focuses on alternative asset management, capital markets, and insurance solutions. Its portfolio spans private equity, credit, real assets, and hedge fund partnerships, and its insurance arm operates through Global Atlantic Financial Group, offering retirement, life, and reinsurance products. For additional information on KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and its strategies, visit the company’s website. Investor Relations contact details are provided there.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) NAMES THREE CO-PRESIDENTS TO SHARPEN EXECUTION AND STRATEGY

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective 22nd April 2026, to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as the firm refines its strategy in a slower macro environment.The appointments signal a structural shift intended to sharpen execution across investment, fundraising, and operational priorities at Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) as rates rise and deal activity slows.Schwartz has emphasized clearer accountability and faster decisions since taking the helm, and the co-presidents model mirrors arrangements seen at other large private equity firms to share oversight of key levers.Investors will watch how the changes influence Carlyle Group's investment activity and quarterly results in the quarters ahead.

+ CARLYLE GROUP TO BUY MAJOR MAI CAPITAL STAKE VALUED AT OVER $2.8B

The Carlyle Group Inc. said on 31st March 2026 that it will acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, in a deal valued at more than $2.8 billion. Under the terms, MAI employees will hold a meaningful minority stake and MAI will continue to operate independently under Rick Buoncore, who serves as chairman and chief executive.The investment is designed to reinforce MAI's capital base and accelerate growth across its services, including financial planning, investment management and family-office capabilities.As of Jan 1, MAI and its affiliates oversee $72.6 billion in assets across 40 offices in the United States, and Carlyle will deploy its global resources to support continuity for clients and advisors.The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. Carlyle has a track record of originating, structuring and leading equity investments in wealth-management platforms. Analysts note that while Carlyle is a solid investment, some investors may prefer AI stocks with greater upside; a report on undervalued AI stocks is available.

+ CARLYLE GROUP SECURES CONTROLLING INTEREST IN MAI CAPITAL VALUED AT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has agreed to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing the adviser at more than $2.8 billion. Under the deal, Carlyle will take controlling ownership while existing owners including Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit; MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake. Carlyle initially invested in MAI in 2021 through Galway Holdings, which had acquired the firm that year. MAI currently manages $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 offices nationwide and employs more than 700 people, a platform the firm says supports a diversified client base across the U.S. financial advisory and asset management landscape. The transaction occurs as Carlyle has faced stock volatility, though the company points to strong liquidity and a long dividend history, with 15 straight years of payouts at roughly 3%. Analysts note Carlyle’s overall health as FAIR, while MAI’s leadership has highlighted cultural alignment as a key driver of the partnership. MAI Chairman and CEO Rick Buoncore commented, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The deal is slated to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, said, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing." Beyond MAI, Carlyle has signaled ongoing deal activity, including a defense-focused fund and a $1 billion investment in Airtel’s data center subsidiary, Nxtra Data Ltd, as well as partnerships with CVC and UBS to broaden product distribution. BMO Capital Markets recently reaffirmed an Outperform rating on Carlyle with a $65 target as investors weigh the firm’s growth trajectory.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL PROVIDES $300 MILLION FINANCING TO ESTABLISHMENT LABS

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. has secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan from Oaktree Capital Management. The facility, finalized on 3rd May 2026, refinances existing debt through 2031 and reserves US$35 million for growth initiatives.The loan carries a fixed-rate, no-amortization structure with flexible prepayment options and a first-priority lien on substantially all assets, enhancing funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues its operational and expansion plans.The investment thesis centers on the Motiva implant platform and minimally invasive procedures expected to drive broad adoption.Management guidance for 2026 projects revenue of US$264-266 million, implying roughly 25-26% growth from the prior year. The financing should ease refinancing pressure and provide liquidity to address losses and cash burn without an immediate equity raise.While the capital raise improves flexibility, there are risks linked to ongoing cash burn and negative free cash flow. Some analysts call for around 30% revenue growth with earnings near US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could reinforce or temper optimism about profitability. The agreement marks a strategic shift in its financing approach.

+ BIOXCEL AND OAKTREE EXTEND REGISTRATION RIGHTS, ISSUE PRIVATE WARRANTS

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed in an 8-K filing that it has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and a group of institutional holders. The move aligns with the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and provides a framework for potential future equity transactions under the arrangement disclosed on 18 April 2026. Key elements of the deal include amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was signed on 27 March 2026, setting the stage for these warrants. On 15 April 2026, the company issued the unregistered warrants in a private placement, a mechanism that avoided immediate Securities Act registration as part of the financing strategy. BioXcel said it would file registration statements with the SEC to enable possible resale of the warrants' underlying shares, a move designed to improve liquidity and maintain investor confidence. Lenders have indicated they intend to hold the securities for investment purposes rather than seek rapid resale, which could moderate near-term volatility in the stock price. Market participants should note the potential dilution risk if warrants are exercised, and the registration process could increase the total share count. The financing approach signals the company's liquidity needs and strategic objectives, rather than an implicit endorsement of aggressive share issuance. BioXcel also clarified that it is not classified as an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting and regulatory requirements.

+ OAKTREE PARTNERS WITH NORTH MILL ON $355.8 MILLION EQUIPMENT FINANCE SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management, in partnership with North Mill Equipment Finance, has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization called OAKEF 2026-1. The closing occurred on 8th April 2026 and signals strong investor appetite for quality equipment finance assets.The collateral consists of equipment finance assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal completed in November 2025.The offering was rated from AAA to single A and attracted peak subscriptions of approximately 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack. The deal delivered a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%.Truist Securities served as the lead structuring agent, with Deutsche Bank acting as co-manager.Brendan Beer, portfolio manager for Oaktree's asset-backed finance and structured credit platform, said the market response reflected investor confidence in Oaktree's equipment strategy and highlighted the North Mill collaboration as a key driver.David Lee, chairman and CEO of North Mill Equipment Finance, stressed that the partnership demonstrates asset quality and the servicing platform's strength, noting that NMEF will continue as primary servicer for the assets.The securitization reinforces Oaktree's position in asset-backed finance and shows its ability to attract significant investor interest and structure deals that meet partner financing objectives.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION INVESTMENT FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT (NYSE:APO) THROUGH CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED SHARES

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., through the issuance of convertible preferred shares that grant Apollo a 13% ownership stake. The transaction was formalized on April 20, 2026 and remains subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The deal values the company at a post-money of approximately $9.62 billion, with the new capital intended to enhance operational capabilities and broaden market reach.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) POSTS 13% MINORITY STAKE IN MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS VIA $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The move aligns with McKesson's strategy to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and provides capital to advance strategic initiatives. The convertible preferred structure allows Apollo to participate in potential upside while McKesson maintains a staged path toward the separation. The investment underscores a strategic alignment between the two firms as McKesson seeks to optimize its core businesses. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, signaling investor confidence in growth opportunities. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells and one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will monitor the implications for McKesson's separation plans and for Apollo's portfolio strategy.

+ APOLLO CLOSES GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP DEAL TO BOLSTER UK BUILD-TO-RENT PLATFORM

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK housing footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a transaction completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed.The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans.GLG is recognized as a leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired more than 5,000 homes.Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios and helping GLG access capital from both Apollo and third-party partners to accelerate development.Gatehouse Bank said the sale will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) RAISES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS FUND; FIRST CLOSE EXPECTED IN SPRING

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, reflecting ongoing investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The fund’s first close is expected to occur in spring, per remarks from Chief Executive Michael Arougheti on an earnings callArougheti described the fund as having "very strong demand" after the solid performance of prior vintages. The fundraising comes more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares Management completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalBeyond Japan, Ares has advanced other capital initiatives, including Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets that include a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, co-president of Ares, said the firm is fundraising to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, largely tied to commitments to its seventh special situations fund, SSG Fund VIIReturning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is reviewing a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account sharing a mandate similar to the SSG series, focused on non performing loans and single-asset distress in Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) EXPANDS RETAIL PLATFORM WITH US$1.7 BILLION WHITESTONE REIT PURCHASE

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) has agreed to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding its footprint in necessity-based retail centers across major U.S. metro areas. The deal signals progress in Ares' strategy to deepen exposure to essential consumer needs within real estate and related assets. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, and appointed Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong to lead the Asia Special Situations team, reflecting the firm's push to grow credit and special situations in Asia. Whitestone's inclusion strengthens Ares' position in retail real estate and aligns with a broader plan to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types, including private credit and real assets. Investors will assess how Whitestone integrates with Ares' existing operations and the progress of Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape. + QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO REOPENS UAE ROUTES, EXPANDS FREIGHTER SCHEDULE

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after a temporary halt due to safety concerns. The updated schedule, published on 28th April 2026, confirms freighter services to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly starting 26th April, and freighter flights to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly beginning 1st May. Daily passenger flights to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with the airline planning to lift freighter destinations to over 50 by 1st May and to expand the passenger network to more than 150 destinations by 16th June.Additionally, shipment restrictions have been lifted on certain urgent freight routes such as Victoria, Australia. Starting 1st May, Qatar Airways Cargo will offer products under special handling codes: QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo; and LHO for live human organs and blood. These codes are designed to support time-sensitive cargo while maintaining safety and compliance.Bookings remain available through the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, while standard sales channels continue to operate. The carrier emphasized that existing products remain accessible during the ramp-up.Analysts and investors are watching the expansion closely as the UAE freight network gains momentum, signaling a broader recovery for regional logistics.This resumption follows earlier safety concerns and underscores the airline's commitment to reviving connectivity and service levels in the Gulf region.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS LAUNCHES NEXT-GEN QSUITE TO LEAD PREMIUM TRAVEL

As of 23rd April 2026, Qatar Airways said the Qsuite Next Generation will debut later this year on the Airbus A350-1000, following delays in the delivery of its new Boeing 777-9 fleet. Originally launched in 2017, the Qsuite has become a hallmark of the airline's premium cabin, with features such as sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating that can accommodate families and small groups. The Next Generation will add a movable 4K OLED screen and enhanced privacy options, while sharpening space management to give travelers more personal space and flexibility during flights. Qatar is positioning the product as part of its broader premium ecosystem at Doha Hamad International Airport, where passengers enjoy dedicated check-in, fast-track security, and access to lounges. The company says the move aims to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure passengers and to keep ahead of rivals who are also pursuing luxury cabin innovations.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS BOOSTS GLOBAL REACH AS NETWORK REBUILD GAINS MOMENTUM

Qatar Airways is continuing to lift embargoes and restore its network; by April 20, 2026, passenger operations are back to over 150 destinations, while freighter services now cover more than 50 locations.The restoration is part of a broader strategy to rebuild capacity and meet growing demand in air cargo.However, certain shipments remain restricted. Special handling codes AVF, AVM, and DOC are still embargoed, and live human organs and blood are not accepted.Customers can still book QRLIV shipments under alternate codes, as long as transit time in Doha does not exceed 24 hours.Other products are available via the Digital Lounge and local sales teams, and Qatar Airways Cargo says the network restoration reflects its adaptability. The carrier is lifting embargoes in phases, balancing safety with capacity and aiming to capture a larger share of the growing air cargo market.

value.Title: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-YCV3-SHN4-P3D5-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: WHITEMAN OSTERMAN AND HANNA LLP ANNOUNCES EMMA MARSHALL AND MATTHEW MOSDELL AS FIRM ASSOCIATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ8-BM03-RV4B-G3SX-00000-00WHITEMAN OSTERMAN AND HANNA LLP ANNOUNCES EMMA MARSHALL AND MATTHEW MOSDELL AS FIRM ASSOCIATES2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisWHITEMAN OSTERMAN AND HANNA LLP ANNOUNCES EMMA MARSHALL AND MATTHEW MOSDELL AS FIRM ASSOCIATESStates News ServiceALBANY, N.Y.

The following information was released by the Capital Region Chamber:

Whiteman Osterman and Hanna LLP, the Capital Region's largest law firm, is pleased to announce that Emma E. Marshall and Matthew D. Mosdell have joined the Firm as Associates.

Emma Marshall joins the Firm's Environmental and Land Use and Development Practice Groups with experience advising clients on complex regulatory, environmental and energy matters. She has represented renewable generation and transmission developers in proceedings before the New York State Department of Public Service, including matters under Articles 10, VII and VIII, and has guided clients through the State Environmental Quality Review Act process. Her experience also includes environmental and regulatory due diligence, hazardous waste compliance and telecommunications regulatory matters.

Prior to joining the Firm, Ms. Marshall was an associate at a regional law firm where she focused on renewable energy and infrastructure development matters.

Ms. Marshall earned her Juris Doctor, summa cum laude, from Albany Law School, where she served as an editor for the Albany Law Review. She received her Bachelor of Arts in Biology from Binghamton University.

Matthew Mosdell joins the Firm's Litigation and Appeals Practice Group. He brings a broad range of experience in civil, criminal and family law matters, with a particular focus on appellate work. Prior to joining the Firm, Mr. Mosdell served as an Appellate Court Attorney with New York's Appellate Division, Third Department, where he prepared reports and draft decisions to assist Justices in resolving complex legal issues across a wide range of cases. His background also includes experience in general litigation, including commercial disputes, personal injury, medical malpractice and estate matters.

Mr. Mosdell earned his Juris Doctor, summa cum laude, from Albany Law School. He also holds a Ph.D. and M.A. in Philosophy from the University of Utah and a B.S. from Utah State University.

urn:contentItem:6JH7-03C3-RS0T-82TD-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - NEWS RECENT INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS-LED FINANCING INJECTS OVER USD 180 MILLION INTO PURE SALMON JAPAN’S LAND-BASED SALMON PROJECT

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is earmarked to support the construction of Pure Salmon Japan’s land-based farming facility and to help meet rising domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores institutional backing for the project, which envisions a facility capable of producing up to 10,000 tonnes and potentially becoming Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. It relies on advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to bolster food security by reducing import dependency.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding presence in Japan, brings capital and expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, described the initiative as a meaningful step for strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS SENIOR HOUSING PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. The deal, completed on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by Fortress affiliates, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018 on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, the property serves a growing, affluent senior population along Florida's northeast coast. Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP ARRANGES $96 MILLION FINANCING FOR JASPER DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES MULTIFAMILY PROJECT

Fortress Investment Group has closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a newly developed 296-unit Class A multifamily community in Downtown Los Angeles. The financing, facilitated by Fortress affiliates, was completed on Feb. 9, 2026, highlighting Fortress's ongoing commitment to supporting high-quality real estate developments.Jasper is owned and developed by Cityview and sits near the University of Southern California campus. The property comprises studios, one- and two-bedroom residences featuring upscale finishes and a comprehensive amenity package, including affordable housing units. Proceeds from the loan will refinance existing construction debt and back the ongoing leasing efforts for the new community.Cityview's Damian Gancman described Fortress's involvement as efficient and solution-oriented, noting a seamless execution from start to finish. Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt at Fortress, said the project aligns with the firm's focus on backing high-quality, newly built multifamily housing with significant sponsor equity. Fortress, founded in 1998, manages about $54 billion in assets as of Sept. 30, 2025, and continues to invest across credit, real estate, private equity and permanent capital strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) PARTNERS WITH ALTERRA TO ACCELERATE GLOBAL CLIMATE INVESTMENTS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Partners With ALTERRA to Accelerate Global Climate InvestmentsKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) secured a commitment from the UAE's ALTERRA, a $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to bolster its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made Apr. 22, 2026, as KKR seeks to mobilize private capital for sustainable energy projects. The terms of the commitment were not disclosed.ALTERRA, launched at COP28 with UAE backing, aims to de-risk climate investments and has already formed partnerships with BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG. With KKR’s inclusion, ALTERRA now counts a broad set of investment expertise and targets up to $250 billion in mobilized climate finance by 2030, a substantial share of which is expected to flow to developing markets.KKR’s Global Climate Transition Strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, spans renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. The firm manages more than $100 billion in assets and has invested over $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable bets include Zenobe in the United Kingdom, EGC in Germany, Avantus in the United States and IGNIS P2X for industrial decarbonization. Gailliot and Lagarrigue highlighted that ALTERRA’s global reach combined with KKR’s infrastructure expertise could scale proven technologies and build more resilient energy systems.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) INVESTS IN STARITAS THROUGH ACCEL-KKR SPIN-OUT

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through its Accel-KKR unit to back the spin-out from ECR as it seeks growth in digital technology markets. The announcement signals KKR's continued focus on technology investments through Accel-KKR and aligns with Staritas' standalone growth plan. Staritas will operate independently from ECR to accelerate product development and market penetration, aided by the resources and expertise of Accel-KKR and KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR). The collaboration aims to position Staritas to respond quickly to evolving customer demands in the digital space. Investors will watch how the Staritas partnership unfolds in a competitive tech landscape, as the management team plans to expand offerings and strengthen service capabilities with this new capital and guidance.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT GLOBAL HEAD OF DIRECT LENDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut Global Head of Direct LendingTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending, effective Apr. 20, 2026. He has led KKR Real Estate's credit business, growing the platform from a seed investment of $400 million in 2015 to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by the end of 2025, and will oversee growth across a broader geographic footprint.Paul Fine, a 2015 hire, is named head of originations to succeed Traut. Fine, previously a managing director, will focus on strengthening client relationships and expanding the firm's capital base. The real estate credit team has grown from 10 employees in 2015 to 130 today, with Ali Imraan leading a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.The leadership transition signals a continued focus on growth and innovation in global real estate credit as KKR looks to capture opportunities across the U.S. and Europe.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ELEVATES THREE EXECUTIVES TO CO-PRESIDENTS TO STRENGTHEN LEADERSHIP

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective from 22nd April 2026. The move aims to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as Carlyle refines its strategy in a challenging economic environment.The three co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and operational management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s franchises, positioning the firm to respond more quickly to market dynamics. By concentrating authority around Schwartz, Carlyle Group seeks greater execution discipline and improved performance in the coming quarters.

+ CARLYLE TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL FOR $2.8 BILLION, EXPANDING WEALTH-MANAGEMENT PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. confirmed on 31st March 2026 that it will acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The deal will transfer majority ownership to Carlyle while MAI employees retain a meaningful minority stake and the firm will continue to operate independently under Rick Buoncore, the chairman and chief executive.The transaction is designed to strengthen MAI's capital base and support growth in its financial planning, investment management and family-office capabilities. As of Jan 1, MAI and its affiliates oversee $72.6 billion in assets across 40 offices in the United States, a scale Carlyle will leverage with its global resources to maintain continuity for clients and advisors.The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary regulatory approvals. Carlyle has a history of originating, structuring and leading equity investments in wealth-management platforms. While some investors may see AI stocks offering greater upside, a report on undervalued AI stocks is available.

+ CARLYLE TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL IN $2.8 BILLION DEAL

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has signed an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing the registered investment adviser at more than $2.8 billion. Under the terms, Carlyle will assume majority ownership, while existing stakeholders including Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital will exit; MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake. Carlyle previously invested in MAI in 2021 through Galway Holdings, which acquired the firm that year. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI manages $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 offices across the United States and has a workforce of more than 700. Despite volatility in Carlyle's stock, the parent firm maintains solid fundamentals, with liquid assets exceeding its near-term obligations and a 15-year streak of dividend payments, currently yielding around 3%. Financial analyses rate Carlyle's overall health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, Chairman and CEO of MAI, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, which facilitated MAI's growth through more than 30 strategic acquisitions during its ownership, said, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK THROUGH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks a milestone in the company's approach to managing its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026 and includes both amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and it introduced provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, granting lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, underscoring the company's financing approach. In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel committed to filing registration statements with the SEC to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This step aims to support liquidity and transparency for investors, with lenders indicating they will hold the securities for investment purposes only. The arrangements could lead to dilution if warrants are exercised and may increase the share count once registered for resale. BioXcel notes it is not an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting requirements; the registration rights agreement, involving several Oaktree entities among other holders, signals continued adherence to securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor forthcoming filings and announcements, as these developments could influence BioXcel's financial position and market performance.

+ OAKTREE AND NORTH MILL CLOSE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization with North Mill Equipment Finance, known as OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026. The deal underscores investor appetite for high-quality equipment finance assets.The securitization pools assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal finalized in November 2025. The offering was rated from AAA to single A and drew peak subscriptions of about 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack with a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%. Truist Securities acted as lead structuring agent and Deutsche Bank served as co-manager.

+ OAKTREE TO HONOR ALL REDEMPTIONS ON ABOUT $7.7 BILLION PRIVATE CREDIT FUND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would honor all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion. Investors will be allowed to redeem up to 8.5% of net assets, or roughly $400 million, with Brookfield contributing about $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund, which targets retail investors, is set to repurchase 6.8% of its own shares, with Brookfield agreeing to buy an additional 1.7% from a single investor, according to a letter to fund holders. The firm is also reducing its quarterly dividend by two cents, bringing the total to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, the fund reported $1.8 billion in liquidity from cash and undrawn facilities. Oaktree has also been selling portions of publicly traded loans and bonds this year to mitigate exposure to software investments, reflecting a broader portfolio-management push during a volatile period. The deal comes amid rising scrutiny of private credit markets, where peers such as Blackstone have faced higher-than-usual repurchase requests and have had to adjust buyback terms. Blackstone's $82 billion private credit fund faced repurchase requests exceeding the typical 5% threshold, prompting the board to raise the buyback to 7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) INVESTS $1.25 BILLION IN MCKESSON CORPORATION (NYSE:MCK) FOR 13% STAKE IN MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS DIVISION

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The investment supports McKesson's plan to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division and is expected to bolster operational efficiencies across the group. McKesson intends to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division, and the capital from Apollo is expected to bolster the strategic plan and fund initiatives across the business. The investment signals a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the company pursues the separation and optimization of its core operations. Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating investors anticipate growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will monitor the deal as it unfolds and its impact on McKesson's separation plans.

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION INVESTMENT FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT (NYSE:APO)

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured a $1.25 billion investment from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc. The deal grants Apollo a 13% stake and was formalized on April 20, 2026 through the issuance of convertible preferred shares, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The funding values Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC at a post-money valuation of approximately $9.62 billion and aims to strengthen operations and expand market presence. Apollo Global Management, Inc., via Apollo Funds, brings strategic backing to the company as demand for surgical solutions grows, with analysts watching how the partnership could influence growth and shareholder value as the transaction proceeds through regulatory review

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT EXPANDS UK PRESENCE WITH GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP ACQUISITION

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a deal completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed. The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans. GLG is a national leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired over 5,000 homes. Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios, enabling GLG to leverage both Apollo capital and third-party partners to accelerate development of high-quality rental housing. Gatehouse Bank's divestiture will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN US$1.7 BILLION TRANSACTION

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding the firm’s footprint in necessity-based retail centers across rapidly growing metropolitan areas in the United States. The deal supports Ares' strategy to diversify across geographies and asset types and align with its emphasis on private credit, real assets, and special situations. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares Management named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring the firm’s focus on strengthening credit and special situations capabilities in Asia. The Whitestone purchase broadens Ares’ exposure to essential retail real estate and supports its strategy to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types. Investors should heed risks including profit margin compression, high debt levels, and a dividend yield of 5.38% that is not fully supported by earnings

+ ARES MANAGEMENT TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN $1.7 BILLION ALL-CASH DEAL

The announcement, made on Apr. 10, 2026, confirms that certain Ares Real Estate funds will acquire all common shares and operating partnership units of Whitestone REIT in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. Whitestone’s portfolio comprises 56 convenience-focused retail properties totaling about 4.9 million square feet in Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. After the deal closes, Whitestone will be private and will cease trading on the NYSE. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman said, "We believe Whitestone has shown the value of high-return smaller spaces occupied by a well-diversified mix of tenants. Our investment strategy is designed to allow businesses to fuel connection and convenience within thriving, dynamic communities. We believe this transaction with Ares is a testament to the value that strategy has created for our business and, ultimately, for our shareholders." David Roth, global head of real estate strategy and growth at Ares Real Estate, added, "This transaction reflects our high conviction in New Economy real estate as today’s consumers are increasingly seeking convenient experiences for their grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness, and dining needs." Ares Management said it expects to support and expand Whitestone's portfolio to create value for communities and investors.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN A $1.7 BILLION DEAL

Ares Management Corp. (NYSE: ARES) has announced a definitive merger agreement to acquire Whitestone REIT (NYSE: WSR) for $1.7 billion, valuing the Houston-based REIT at $19 per share and taking it private. The deal is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026.On 9th April 2026, Ares highlighted that Whitestone’s portfolio offers an appealing opportunity to expand its real estate footprint with a focus on necessity-based retail centers located in high-demand areas of Arizona and Texas. The REIT operates 56 retail properties across approximately 3.9 million square feet, including locations in Phoenix, Austin, Dallas-Fort Worth, Houston, and San Antonio.The acquisition has received unanimous approval from Whitestone’s board of trustees and is contingent upon customary closing conditions, including shareholder approval. Whitestone’s management will continue to see value from the transaction. Whitestone's financial advisory team includes BofA Securities and Jones Lang LaSalle Securities, while Ares Management is advised by Citigroup Global Markets Inc. and Morgan Stanley & Co. This acquisition comes as Whitestone shares have risen, closing at $16.94 on April 8, 2026, up nearly 35 percent from $12.57 a year earlier. As of December 2025, Ares Management held approximately $623 billion in assets under management globally. While shares of Ares closed at $104.83 on April 8, 2026, reflecting a 24 percent decline from $137.75 the previous year, the acquisition of Whitestone REIT could bolster its portfolio and improve investor sentiment moving forward.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESUMES UAE FREIGHT OPERATIONS FOLLOWING SAFETY REVIEW

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed its freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after a temporary halt due to safety concerns. As of 28th April 2026, the carrier published its new schedule, which includes the resumption of freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly, starting from 26th April. In addition, freighter services to Sharjah (SHJ) will operate three times weekly beginning on 1st May, while daily passenger flights to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with plans to expand freighter destinations to more than 50 by 1st May. The passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June.Shipment restrictions have also been lifted on certain urgent freight services to routes such as Victoria, Australia, with Qatar Airways Cargo offering products under new handling codes starting 1st May. The QRLIV codes cover commodities such as fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, while LHO codes cover the transport of live human organs and blood.Reservations remain accessible through the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, in addition to the standard sales channels. The airline says it remains committed to maintaining robust service levels as it ramps back up its operations.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS REVEALS NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE TO ELEVATE BUSINESS CLASS

Qatar Airways has announced the upcoming Qsuite Next Generation, slated to launch later this year after delays in its Boeing 777-9 arrivals. The upgraded business-class product will first appear on the Airbus A350-1000, as of 23rd April 2026. Building on the long-running Qsuite introduced nearly a decade ago, the Next Generation preserves sliding doors, double beds, and configurable seating while integrating advanced technology and improved space management to appeal to business travelers seeking privacy and flexibility. The move underscores Qatar Airways' strategy to lead premium travel, leveraging Doha Hamad International Airport's reputation and its premium ecosystem, including dedicated check-in, fast-track security, and lounge access.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS NETWORK AS RESTORATIONS CONTINUE

Qatar Airways has been expanding its operations, with belly and freighter services increasing to and from Doha. As of April 20, 2026, the carrier has restored its passenger network to more than 150 destinations and expanded freighter coverage to over 50 locations worldwide.The network restoration is part of a broader plan to rebuild capacity in the air cargo market. While the network grows, some shipments remain embargoed; AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes are restricted, and live organs and blood are not accepted. Customers can still book QRLIV shipments under alternative handling codes, provided the Doha transit time does not exceed 24 hours. All other products remain bookable through standard channels, including the Digital Lounge and local sales teams, underscoring the airline's adaptability to rising demand and shifting logistics conditions.
