Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGY-J4J3-RTN5-V038-00000-00-Soluna Holdings Regains Compliance with Nasdaq Listing Requirements2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexis-Soluna Holdings Regains Compliance with Nasdaq Listing Requirements

ALBANY, N.Y. - Soluna Holdings, Inc. ('Soluna' or the 'Company') (NASDAQ: SLNH), a developer of green data centers for intensive computing applications such as AI and Bitcoin mining, today announced that it has received notice from The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC ('Nasdaq') that the Company has regained compliance with the minimum bid price requirement under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).



In regaining compliance with the Listing Rule, the Company's shares maintained a minimum closing bid price of $ 1.00 per share or greater from April 14, 2026, to April 29, 2026. As a result, Nasdaq has closed the matter.

'We've done the work to meet Nasdaq's requirements, and more importantly, to strengthen the business underneath it,' said John Belizaire, CEO of Soluna Holdings. 'Our focus remains on disciplined execution, scaling our Renewable Computing platform, and creating durable value for shareholders.'

Soluna is now in full compliance with all Nasdaq continued listing requirements, and the Company's stock remains listed and traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker 'SLNH.'

Safe Harbor Statement by Soluna This announcement contains forward-looking statements. These statements are made under the 'safe harbor' provisions of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These forward-looking statements can be identified by terminology such as 'will,' 'expects,' 'anticipates,' 'future,' 'intends,' 'plans,' 'believes,' 'estimates,' 'confident,' and similar statements. Other examples of forward-looking statements may include, but are not limited to, statements of the Company's plans and objectives. The Company may also make written or oral forward-looking statements in its periodic reports to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ('SEC'), in its annual report to shareholders, in press releases and other written materials, and in oral statements made by its officers, directors, or employees to third parties. Statements that are not historical facts, including but not limited to statements about the Company's beliefs and expectations, are forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements involve inherent risks and uncertainties; further information regarding which is included in the Company's filings with the SEC. All information provided in this press release is as of the press release date, and the Company undertakes no duty to update such information, except as required under applicable law.

About Soluna Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: SLNH)

Soluna is on a mission to make renewable energy a global superpower, using computing as a catalyst. The Company designs, develops, and operates digital infrastructure that transforms surplus renewable energy into global computing resources. Soluna's pioneering data centers are strategically co-located with wind, solar, or hydroelectric power plants to support high-performance computing applications, including Bitcoin Mining, Generative AI, and other compute-intensive applications. Soluna's proprietary software MaestroOS() helps energize a greener grid while delivering cost-effective and sustainable computing solutions and superior returns. To learn more, visit solunacomputing.com and follow us on:

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/solunaholdings/ X (formerly Twitter): x.com/solunaholdings YouTube: youtube.com/c/solunacomputing Newsletter: bit.ly/solunasubscribe Resource Center: solunacomputing.com/resources

Soluna regularly posts important information on its website and encourages investors and potential investors to consult the Soluna investor relations and investor resources sections of its website regularly.

Contact:

Public Relations
West of Fairfax for Soluna
Soluna@westof.co

[Editorial queries for this story should be sent to newswire@enpublishing.co.uk ]

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH4-65J3-RRKY-D3RN-00000-00MILBANK LLP - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKMILBANK LLP - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK PROPOSES PHASED, LONG-TERM PROTECTION FOR ASH CANOPY

Milbank is deploying a strategic plan to combat the emerald ash borer, aiming to protect its ash trees from nearly half of its canopy. The infestation, detected last summer, threatens trees in parks and the cemetery, with removal costs projected up to $4 million.The city’s alternative is a 'herd immunity' program, estimated at $2.4 million over 12 years, centered on targeted treatments and ongoing monitoring. Officials say the approach seeks to preserve the urban forest while reducing beetle spread.The plan relies on cooperation among local leaders, environmental experts, and residents, with a phased rollout prioritizing vulnerable areas. Milbank hopes the initiative may offer a replicable model for other communities facing the same pest challenge.

+ MILBANK LAWYERS EXPLAIN CFTC'S NEW GUIDANCE ON PREDICTION MARKETS AND ITS LEGAL RAMIFICATIONS

Milbank analyzed the CFTC’s new guidance on prediction markets in a recent webinar, which followed the agency’s 17th March 2026 announcement introducing a regulatory expectations advisory and an Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking with a 45-day public comment period ending 30th April.Partner Josh Sterling highlighted the rapid growth of prediction markets, rising from $3 billion in trading volume in January 2025 to $27 billion in January 2026, drawing attention from market participants, courts, and state regulators. The event also featured Milbank litigation partners Grant Mainland, Matt Laroche, and John Williams.Laroche said the advisory signals the CFTC’s belief that sports-event contracts fall within its jurisdiction, a notable clarification. He added that the advisory acknowledges prediction markets as a distinct financial asset class that is expanding and can provide useful information. Williams pointed to the rulemaking notice as a strong stance by the CFTC to claim jurisdiction, while leaving future steps less defined, noting the questions for public comment on hedging, price risk, and information dissemination.Milbank suggested practical steps for operators, including reviewing listings for compliance, engaging with leagues, mitigating manipulation risks, participating in rulemaking, and watching blockchain developments. Mainland also noted more than 20 active court cases involving prediction markets, illustrating a legal environment that remains complex and unsettled, especially regarding whether the Commodity Exchange Act preempts state law.

+ MILBANK FACILITATES KENNEDY-WILSON BONDHOLDER DISCUSSION AMID INVESTOR INTEREST

Milbank LLP will host a conference call for holders of Kennedy-Wilson, Inc.'s 4.75% notes due 2029, 4.75% notes due 2030, and 5.00% notes due 2031 on 5th March 2026 at 3:30 p.m. (EST) to discuss questions arising from the Exchange Offer and consent solicitation statement dated 2nd March 2026.The firm has already seen indications of interest from holders representing a substantial portion of the notes, suggesting meaningful investor engagement.Those wishing to participate should provide their contact information and holdings data on a series-by-series basis. Milbank, a New York-based firm with more than 150 years of history and offices worldwide, offers cross-practice services to commercial, financial, and industrial clients, including institutions and governments. Milbank is also reviewing developments affecting unsecured creditors of Braskem S.A., illustrating its broad advisory scope and emphasis on transparent issuer-investor dialogue.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON POSTS 40% REVENUE RISE IN LONDON, SIGNALING STRONG DEMAND

Debevoise &Plimpton reported a 40% revenue rise in its London office, reaching $268 million, with the results released on 7th April 2026.The increase reflects rising demand for corporate law and dispute resolution services, aided by the firm's focus on high-value client engagements and market-adaptive capabilities.Executives say the firm will continue investing in talent and expanding offerings to maintain momentum, a move that could attract more clients and sustain growth.Analysts note that the legal sector is evolving rapidly and that continued strong performance will depend on sustaining innovation and client service in a competitive environment.

+ YPF WINS APPELLATE VICTORY IN $16 BILLION EXPROPRIATION DISPUTE

The case traces back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and expropriation of 51 percent of YPF. Litigation began in 2015, and Debevoise took over YPF's defense in December 2020 after earlier motions to dismiss were denied. After two years of extensive discovery and expert testimony, the parties sought summary judgment in 2022, and in 2023 the district court granted YPF's motion in full while largely granting the plaintiffs against the Republic, resulting in a judgment exceeding $16 billion against the Republic in September 2023.

+ DEBEVOISE STRENGTHENS EUROPEAN DISPUTES LEADERSHIP WITH SULLIVAN KC AND INA POPOVA

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has appointed Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately, expanding the firm's leadership in key markets including Paris, Frankfurt and Luxembourg.The firm said the appointments, announced on the 17th of March, 2026, align with its strategy to strengthen international dispute resolution capabilities across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, Co-Chair of the global International Dispute Resolution Group, framed the move as building on Debevoise's long-standing strength in major disputes across Europe and maintaining high standards of advocacy and counsel.Sullivan KC is known for leadership in international arbitration and public international law and will continue handling high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations; Popova brings broad experience in cross-border disputes with dual civil and common-law qualifications and fluency in several languages; Debevoise's IDR Group has four decades of experience and a track record spanning more than 125 countries and matters totaling over $110 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ FROM REGULATION TO CRYPTO: RODGE COHEN’S LONG VIEW ON BANKING’S FUTURE

H. Rodgin "Rodge" Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has helped shape banking policy for more than five decades. On 19th February 2026, at an NYU Law Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance event, he reflected on the evolution of the financial system, with remarks from Jay Clayton and Edward Rock providing context on today’s regulatory landscape.Cohen cited landmark regulatory shifts, including the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, which broadened access to banking and redefined compliance, contributing to a drop in the unbanked rate from roughly one in three to five percent. Clayton noted the regulation changes since the 1970s, while Cohen’s advocacy supported interstate banking moves in the 1980s that reshaped the industry’s geography.He recalled the 2008 crisis, representing major banks as policymakers acted to stabilize markets. Cohen described the tense hours before the government’s intervention after Lehman Brothers’ collapse, saying, "We were on the edge of a cliff," a moment that underscored the urgency of decisive action and cemented his standing as a premier financial-services lawyer.Looking ahead, Cohen warned that cryptocurrency regulation must keep pace with rapid market evolution. He emphasized that regulators must avoid missteps, citing Gresham’s Law that "bad regulation drives out good regulation" and underscoring Sullivan & Cromwell’s ongoing role in guiding the sector’s future.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL ADDS MCLAUGHLIN AND MCVEIGH TO LONDON OFFICE TO STRENGTHEN PRIVATE CAPITAL COUNSEL

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP announced two prominent hires for its London office—Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh—to bolster its private capital capabilities in the UK and Europe. Dated Jan. 21, 2026, the firm said McLaughlin will lead London’s private equity sponsor and borrower acquisitions and leveraged finance, while McVeigh will steer the European private credit offering, focusing on direct and specialty lending.The firm’s Co-Chairs, Robert Giuffra and Scott Miller, welcomed the appointments, saying the pair’s expertise will expand the firm’s ability to serve private equity sponsors, lenders, and private credit providers across Europe and beyond. McLaughlin is noted for extensive experience in acquisition and leveraged finance, with recognition from Chambers UK and Legal 500. McVeigh is similarly esteemed for his work in leveraged finance and private credit, enhancing Sullivan & Cromwell’s growing practice in this area.Speaking from the London office, co-managing partners Kon Asimacopoulos and John Horsfield-Bradbury underscored that the hires are central to delivering the firm’s private capital strategy while leveraging its global platform and established U.S. finance practices.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ FRESHFIELDS STRENGTHENS US CORPORATE LITIGATION WITH WILLKIE’S MARY EATON

Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer is expanding its US corporate litigation capabilities with the hire of Mary Eaton from Willkie Farr & Gallagher, who will co head the securities and shareholder litigation group with Meredith Kotler.Eaton's background includes leading Willkie's corporate litigation practice and representing clients such as CBS, Facebook and Merck & Co. The move follows Freshfields' earlier recruitment of Cleary Gottlieb partners.Ethan Klingsberg noted the strong mix of Eaton and Kotler as it positions the firm for rising shareholder litigation and a rebound in M&A.While acknowledging the departure of M&A head Mitchell Presser to Morrison & Foerster, Freshfields remains focused on growing its domestic team and expanding its footprint in the US. The firm also posted a 1% rise in profits to £688 million last year.

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER EARNS INTERNATIONAL AI STANDARDS CERTIFICATION

Willkie Farr & Gallagher has earned certification under international AI standards, highlighting the firm's commitment to responsible technology in law. The certification was awarded on 14th April 2026.The firm is recognized for adhering to globally accepted AI principles, including transparency, accountability, and ethical use of technology in legal processes. This achievement is expected to reinforce client confidence as demand grows for ethically governed AI use.The certification process involved rigorous assessments and compliance checks, with Willkie Farr & Gallagher implementing robust protocols to manage AI tools and mitigate deployment risks. The move positions the firm to compete for tech-focused clients.Experts suggest the certification could set a precedent for other firms, encouraging a broader industry shift toward AI governance and ethical standards in legal services.

+ WILLKIE STRENGTHENS GERMANY PLATFORM WITH PARTNER HIRE SEBASTIAN HAFELE IN MUNICH

Willkie Farr & Gallagher has appointed Dr. Sebastian Hafele as a partner in Germany, a move that strengthens the Munich team and fits the firm's strategy to expand its German M&A and private equity capabilities. Based in Munich, Hafele specializes in M&A, private equity, public takeovers, and corporate reorganizations, and he joins on 23 December 2025 from Kirkland & Ellis with experience on German and cross-border deals. The hire accompanies Willkie's broader German growth push, which has included bringing in lateral partners in private equity, antitrust and litigation and opening a Hamburg office, giving Willkie three German offices alongside Munich and Frankfurt. Kamyar Abrar, Co-Managing Partner for Germany, welcomed Hafele, saying his experience will strengthen Willkie's client service and transactional capabilities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB [3 News That Matters]

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB TAPS FORMER AMAZON AI GOVERNANCE LEAD TO BOLSTER FINTECH PRACTICE

Cleary Gottlieb announced on 23rd April 2026 the reappointment of Igor Kleyman as a partner in its financial institutions practice, reinforcing capabilities in digital assets, AI, and fintech.Kleyman returns after nearly ten years advising on digital assets and AI, including a recent role at Amazon leading Responsible AI Governance, with prior positions at Nasdaq and Gemini Trust Company addressing regulatory challenges and product development.The firm highlighted its multidisciplinary digital assets group as the 2025 Law360 Practice Group of the Year, noting its work in shaping regulatory frameworks and enforcement actions for digital asset clients. Kleyman's governance and compliance experience is expected to complement the team's standards-setting work.Karpf commented, "Igor's background across fintech, AI, and regulation will strengthen Cleary's position at the forefront of financial services law," and Kleyman added, "I look forward to collaborating across Cleary's global network to advance our relationships with fintechs and digital asset innovators."

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB BOLSTERS FINTECH AND REGULATORY CAPABILITIES WITH RETURN OF AMAZON AI AND DIGITAL ASSETS AUTHORITY

Cleary Gottlieb is adding back a digital assets and AI specialist who previously held a senior role at Amazon. The return is expected to bolster the firm's capabilities in blockchain, cryptocurrency regulations, and AI implications in finance. On 20th April 2026, the firm confirmed the specialist's reappointment, aligning with its strategy to grow the fintech and regulatory advisory footprint. The move signals Cleary Gottlieb's proactive stance in a fast-evolving legal landscape.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB ROLLS OUT CX+ TO MODERNIZE IN-HOUSE LEGAL OPS

Cleary Gottlieb has introduced CX+, a AI-powered platform designed to improve legal workflows through contract intelligence and due diligence. The system, announced on the 15th of April 2026, comprises CX+Insights and CX+Transact to serve in-house teams and deal professionals.Developed with input from senior in-house clients and legal operations experts, CX+ is configurable to fit diverse workflows and can be integrated with existing document management systems via custom APIs. The platform emphasizes security and model-agnostic operation, allowing firms to tailor CX+ to their stacks.CX+ Insights enables loading and querying of contracts with AI-driven bundles and dashboards, continually updating analytics as new documents arrive. CX+ Transact supports contract review and M&A due diligence with tagging, filtering and in-place editing, plus real-time progress visibility.Early data show notable efficiency gains, with time and cost savings of 40-60% on due diligence tasks across more than 150 deals.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LATHAM & WATKINS AND LOEB & LOEB SUPPORT AI BATTERY COMPANY IN SIGNIFICANT SPAC FINANCING

Two leading law firms are guiding a $250 million SPAC merger that would bring AI Battery Company to the public markets. The arrangement is aimed at boosting the battery maker’s capital resources and supporting its growth in AI-driven energy storage technologies.The transaction was announced on 21st April 2026 and focuses on advancing AI-enabled battery solutions while expanding R&D and manufacturing capabilities.Latham & Watkins and Loeb & Loeb provide SPAC and regulatory governance expertise, positioning AI Battery Company for a smooth transition to public ownership. The deal remains subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals.Industry observers say the deal highlights strong demand for AI-integrated technologies and could accelerate momentum for AI Battery Company in a competitive market.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ACHIEVES $8.3 BILLION REVENUE, PEP UP 21%

Latham & Watkins LLP reported fiscal year 2025 results with global gross revenue of $8.3 billion, an 18.6% rise from 2024, and the figures were released on April 16, 2026 alongside the Am Law 100 rankings. Profit per equity partner reached about $8.7 million, up 21.3% from roughly $7.1 million in 2024.The London office surpassed $1 billion in revenue for the first time, up from about $850 million in 2024, a growth of more than 17%. With around 1,000 staff, it remains the firm’s largest non-U.S. base and a key pillar of its European growth strategy.Core drivers include M&A, private equity, finance, and capital markets, with nearly 800 deals in 2025 valued at more than $720 billion. Revenue per lawyer is estimated at about $2.23 million based on roughly 3,700 lawyers; overall headcount supports a favorable margin backdrop as Am Law 100 trends show continued momentum for top firms.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS POSTS RECORD $8.3 BILLION REVENUE AMID BROAD MARKET DEMAND

Latham &Watkins reported $8.3 billion in global revenue on 15th April 2026, a firm record signaling strong market momentum.The performance reflects demand across major practices, notably in capital markets, private equity, and high-stakes regulatory matters, with a diversified portfolio sustaining growth amid volatility.The revenue mix benefited from a global platform and high-leverage model, with activity in restructuring and finance adding balance; the firm is expected to continue hiring and competing on compensation to attract top talent.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [3 News That Matters]

+ LONDON GROWTH REFLECTS SKADDEN'S MOVE TO NON-EQUITY PARTNER MODEL

Skadden has promoted nine London partners in a 55-strong global round, its first since introducing a non-equity partner tier. The cohort this year more than doubles last year's 22, with London's intake up from the City's historically small rounds.The nine London appointees cover a range of practices including White Collar Defense and Investigations, Finance, Corporate, Tax, National Security, Financial Institutions and Capital Markets. They are Jonathan Benson, Zoe Q. Cooper Sutton, Benjamin T. Davies, Kara L. Heggs, Jason Hewitt, Nicola Kerr-Shaw, Feargal Ryan, Kenneth J. Ryan and Cristina Tomassini.

+ SKADDEN GUIDES OTSUKA THROUGH $1.2B TRANSCEND THERAPEUTICS DEAL AMID BIOTECH M&A SURGE

Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom is advising Tokyo-based Otsuka Pharmaceutical on its acquisition of clinical-stage biotechnology company Transcend Therapeutics in a deal potentially valued at USD1.2 billion. As of 1st April 2026, the transaction is set to bolster Otsuka's portfolio in the competitive biotech landscape.Under the terms of the agreement, Otsuka will pay USD700 million to Transcend shareholders upon completion, anticipated in the second quarter of 2026. In addition, Otsuka could pay up to USD525 million in contingent consideration based on future sales milestones tied to assets currently in development.Transcend is being advised by New York-based Kirkland & Ellis, led by partners Graham Robinson and Laura Knoll, with support from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman partner Ron Fleming Jr. Centerview Partners is handling financial advisory duties for Transcend, with Andrew S Rymer and Jason Zuckerbrod, alongside principal Tommaso Verderame. The Skadden engagement highlights the biotech M&A trend as companies seek to strengthen capabilities amid regulatory complexities and evolving market dynamics.

+ STRATEGIC FLORA FOODS ACQUISITION BY ALICORP SIGNALS REGIONAL GROWTH, BACKED BY ELITE LAW FIRMS

Simpson Thacher and Skadden have advised Alicorp on acquiring Flora Food Group's brand portfolio, extending its footprint into 17 Latin American markets (excluding Mexico and Brazil) and securing a manufacturing facility in Cali, Colombia. The deal was announced on 18th March 2026.The move aligns with Alicorp's strategy to diversify its offerings and strengthen its competitive position by bringing in established brands and new manufacturing capabilities.The same law firms were active on other Latin American deals, including Skadden's work for JX Advanced Metals on the sale of Chilean assets to Lundin Mining for $215 million, and A&O Shearman's representation of IHS Mauritius BR Limited in selling Latin American tower operations to Macquarie Asset Management for $952 million.Analysts say the deal reflects a broader trend of consolidation in the region, with investors watching for how such growth moves affect Alicorp's long-term performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 News That Matters]

+ PRIVATE EQUITY PUSH IN JAPAN: KKR MOVES TO TAKE TAIYO HOLDINGS PRIVATE

KKR plans to take Taiyo Holdings private for 528.56 billion yen (roughly $3.3 billion) through a tender offer, with the transaction announced on 10th April 2026. The offer price is 4,750 yen per share, representing a 4.7% discount to Taiyo's last closing price. Key supporters include DIC Corp, Kowa Co and Oasis Management, which together own about 42.2% of Taiyo's outstanding shares.Taiyo Holdings is a global player in electronic materials and pharmaceuticals. The deal reflects growing interest from private equity in Japan as investors seek to expand Taiyo's capabilities and market reach in dynamic industries.The KKR move is being guided by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett, whose M&A team is led by Noritaka Kumamoto and Jonathan Stradling, with Daisuke Ueta and Kaoruko Sakuramoto. The firm is also handling antitrust considerations with Etienne Renaudeau and associates, and credit-related matters with Makiko Harunari, Atsushi Usui and Mark Watson.

+ SIMPSON THACHER HELPS NSSK SECURE LARGE OVERSUBSCRIBED NSSK IV FUNDS

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett advised Nippon Sangyo Suishin Kiko (NSSK) on forming the NSSK IV Series Funds, securing total commitments of JPY250 billion (USD1.5 billion). The milestone was reached on 10th April 2026, and the fund offering closed on 31st March 2026.Guidance from a global team centered on regulatory and compliance matters critical to the fund's formation. Key practitioners included David Azcue, co-head of the private funds practice in Japan; Adam Furber, co-head of the Asia offices and head of the Asia investment funds practice; and Makiko Harunari, head of the Asia banking and credit practice.The NSSK IV Series Funds were more than twice oversubscribed, drawing commitments from over 100 institutional investors worldwide, highlighting strong appetite for NSSK's strategy and for private equity in the Asia-Pacific region.

+ CAPITAL STRUCTURE SOLUTIONS PRACTICE STRENGTHENED BY ADDITION OF BAE AND RUBY

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett said it has added Christine Bae and Jacob Ruby to its Capital Structure Solutions Practice, reinforcing its capabilities in complex financing and restructuring following the practice's early-2026 formation. The hires were announced in a release dated 18th March 2026 and reflect the firm's ongoing push to provide comprehensive capital-structure advice.Led by David Nemecek, the practice aims to deliver coordinated guidance across liability management, private credit, and special situations as it grows with its new partners.Bae and Ruby bring experience advising sponsors, companies, lenders, and investors on intricate capital-structure challenges, including liability management exercises and distressed transactions, with a focus on bespoke solutions for clients facing strategic or financial hurdles. "Christine and Jacob bring deep technical capability, sound judgment, and the ability to craft bespoke solutions for clients navigating their most complex capital structure challenges," said Nemecek. Both new partners expressed enthusiasm for Simpson Thacher's collaborative culture and integrated platform.

+ WACHTELL, LIPTON, ROSEN & KATZ LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ JONES DAY AND WACHTELL LEAD ANTITRUST COUNSEL IN SYSCO'S $29B MOVE ON JETRO

Sysco is pursuing the $29 billion acquisition of Jetro Restaurant Depot with legal support from Jones Day for antitrust counsel and Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP to handle the merger, confirmed on 31st March 2026.The move aims to reinforce Sysco’s market leadership in a sector facing cost pressures and supply-chain challenges. Jetro’s network in the restaurant-supply space could boost Sysco’s operations and customer reach, though regulators will scrutinize the deal given its scale.Jones Day’s antitrust capability and Wachtell’s pedigree in big mergers will shape the regulatory strategy as authorities review the transaction. The firms’ collaboration highlights how major consolidations in the food distribution industry depend on experienced legal navigation.As the industry’s largest distributor, Sysco’s aggressive expansion underscores a broader consolidation trend intended to improve efficiency and service. The deal’s outcome may influence competitive dynamics and future merger activity in the sector.

+ PUBLIC STORAGE EXPANDS SELF-STORAGE PLATFORM WITH $10.5 BILLION ALL-STOCK PURCHASE OF NATIONAL STORAGE AFFILIATES

Public Storage announced on 24th March 2026 that it would acquire National Storage Affiliates in a $10.5 billion all-stock transaction, creating a larger platform for its self-storage portfolio. The deal is structured as an all-stock acquisition and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026 following the formation of a joint venture involving 313 properties valued at about $3.3 billion.Under the terms, NSA shareholders will receive 0.14 Public Storage common shares for each NSA share, valuing the consideration at $41.68 per share.The financing for Public Storage is being led by DLA Piper, providing real estate financing counsel for the transaction. Wachtell Lipton is advising Public Storage, with leadership from Adam Emmerich and Kyle Diamond, while Clifford Chance acts for NSA, with Chang-Do Gong and Robert Chung leading the team, joined by Andrew Epstein and Jeanne Roig-Irwin.

+ X CORP WITHDRAWS HIGH-STAKES $90M CASE VS WACHTELL LIPTON ROSEN & KATZ

Elon Musk’s X Corp has voluntarily dismissed its $90 million lawsuit against Wachtell Lipton Rosen & Katz, ending a protracted dispute from the chaotic Twitter takeover of 2022. Filed in San FranciscoSuperior Court on October 1, 2023, the suit alleged Wachtell pocketed an 'improper bonus payment' that violated fiduciary duties by inflating fees with 'success' bonuses just before the $44 billion deal closed.Wachtell Lipton, which had defended the old Twitter board as it fought to compel Musk to close the buyout, defended itself with vigor. The firm underscored its 'complete success' after months of litigation, noting that the fees at issue were approved by the board, a point that undermines X’s assertions.Reuters was cited in a court filing that disclosed the dismissal, which bars refiling and signifies a clean break between the two parties. A Wachtell Lipton spokesperson commented, 'We are pleased that X has dismissed its meritless lawsuit with prejudice. There was no settlement.' This development marks a significant pivot in the legal saga tied to the Twitter acquisition.The retreat comes close on the heels of another settlement for X Corp, which has acknowledged a $128 million executive payout suit tied to the turbulent acquisition period. The dismissal could indicate a shift in X’s strategy as it moves away from the financial and legal entanglements of its predecessor.As the legal landscape evolves for Elon Musk's X Corp, this resolution with Wachtell Lipton may pave the way for a tighter strategic focus on core operations, with investors and market watchers tracking the implications for the company’s trajectory.

+ CRAVATH, SWAINE & MOORE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CRAVATH ALUM FORMS FOCUSED CORPORATE INVESTIGATIONS BOUTIQUE

A Cravath Swaine & Moore partner is launching a boutique firm dedicated to corporate investigations, reflecting a trend toward specialized services for complex regulatory challenges. The new practice will offer internal investigations, compliance assessments, and risk-management guidance derived from extensive high-stakes experience.On 9th March 2026, the debut signals a broader movement of seasoned attorneys leaving traditional firms to operate more nimble, practice-focused entities. The boutique seeks to bring Cravath-level rigor to clients needing tailored, discreet investigations.Industry observers expect a varied clientele, including Fortune 500 companies, private equity portfolios, and startups. The partner’s network and reputation are anticipated to help establish early credibility and a distinctive market presence.

+ BIOCON'S BIOSIMILARS CONSOLIDATION TIES UP WITH MAJOR LEGAL COUNSEL ACROSS GLOBAL FIRMS

Biocon announced a $1.2 billion deal to wrap up the consolidation of its biosimilars business by acquiring the remaining stakes from minority partners, a move disclosed on December 11, 2025. The transaction, which includes Viatris's exit and stakes from other shareholders, is designed to streamline operations and strengthen Biocon's leadership as biosimilars growth accelerates.Viatris is selling its remaining stake for about $815 million, while True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II are among the other sellers. Serum Institute Life Sciences also took part in the stake sale. Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas advised Serum Institute, and Khaitan & Co and Cravath, Swaine & Moore supported Viatris on financing components. Veritas Legal advised True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II.Biocon's advisory was led by Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas, with Raghubir Menon and Natashaa Shroff guiding the firm’s work, joined by Tarumoy Chaudhuri and Kriti Saraswat. The deal is part of Biocon's broader plan to complete its integration with Viatris and refinance debt, aiming to solidify its leadership in a competitive biosimilars market.The transaction highlights a cross-border legal effort and reflects the involvement of several firms to address financing, regulatory, and commercial considerations as Biocon positions itself for future growth.

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE ROLLS OUT YEAR-END BONUSES, CAP AT $140,000

Cravath Swaine &Moore has rolled out its year-end bonuses, with total potential payouts reaching $140,000 for associates. The New York-based firm notified its staff via an internal memo on 20th November 2025 that standard bonuses span from $15,000 for first-year associates to $115,000 for the most senior, as reported by Above the Law.In addition to the standard bonuses, Cravath will grant special bonuses from $6,000 to $25,000, mirroring Milbank’s recent payouts. The strategy underscores Cravath’s role as a traditional first mover in the pay race for associates. The firm declined to comment further, but the stated structure remains in line with prior years.Following the release, other major firms have already begun to respond. Paul Hastings quickly disclosed its own year-end and special bonuses, which align with Cravath’s scale. A Paul Hastings spokesperson chose not to comment, indicating the firm may be following Cravath’s lead.The Cravath bonuses align with the patterns observed at large U.S. law firms last year, with many associates earning annual salaries between $225,000 and $435,000 depending on class year. The drive to maintain talent amid rivalry in the sector makes such compensation parity common, and Cravath’s decision is likely to influence peers.Looking forward, the U.S. legal market is expected to stay healthy in 2025, driven by stronger client demand at both large and midsize firms, particularly in transactional areas. A recent analysis of firm financial data noted the positive outlook, underscoring the role of competitive pay packages like Cravath’s in shaping the market.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ HONG KONG LISTING OF SIGENERGY REFLECTS GROWING DEMAND FOR ENERGY STORAGE SOLUTIONS

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems developer, has completed its Hong Kong IPO, raising HK$4.4 billion (about $562 million). The listing was supported by leading law firms Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, which provided strategic legal counsel throughout. The deal was recorded on the 17th of April 2026. Founded in May 2022 and headquartered in Shanghai, Sigenergy concentrates on distributed energy storage systems and delivers stackable, solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions. The company's strong market position is reflected in a 28.6 percent share of the global market for stackable, all-in-one DESS products, a figure cited by market data. The offering was underwritten by a syndicate including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation acting as joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters came from Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ team led by Christine Xu and Lipton Li, assisted by counsel Queenie Tong. The deal comes as other infrastructure and energy-related transactions unfold in the region, such as Mindspace Business Parks REIT's 30 billion rupees Chennai IT Park acquisition advised by Khaitan & Co, and SBI Funds Management's planned 130-billion-rupee IPO with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising. The Sigenergy IPO reflects rising investor interest in energy storage and renewables as the sector expands.

+ LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL SECURES $2.5 BILLION SIXTH FUND AMID DAVIS POLK COLLABORATION

Lightyear Capital, co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has closed $2.5 billion for its sixth fund, announced on April 14, 2026.The fund targets financial services and technology, aiming to capitalize on opportunities in those sectors.Davis Polk's involvement extends beyond advisory work; its attorneys have helped structure the fund and navigate regulatory requirements.As fundraising remains competitive, Lightyear's sizable backing underscores confidence in its strategy while the firm seeks to deploy capital and drive returns.

+ DAVIS POLK OPENS LA HUB AS PART OF CALIFORNIA EXPANSION

Davis Polk & Wardwell disclosed on 14th April 2026 that it will launch a Los Angeles office with Jason Russell leading the expansion.Russell is a veteran litigator who ran Skadden's Los Angeles office and served on its policy committee, and he has represented clients such as the NBA, Christie’s and MGA Entertainment.The move aims to broaden the firm's West Coast footprint and deepen its capabilities in a competitive California market.Davis Polk plans to hire for the new office and expects Russell's connections to help attract talent and high-profile assignments.

value.Title: The Swig Company Reduces GHG Emissions and Advances Decarbonization Strategy

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-BDN3-S80C-G525-00000-00The Swig Company Reduces GHG Emissions and Advances Decarbonization Strategy2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisThe Swig Company Reduces GHG Emissions and Advances Decarbonization Strategy

Key Highlights:

* Reduced GHG emissions by 33% across managed property portfolio since 2018.

* Achieved 27% reduction in energy consumption in 2025 compared to 2018 baseline.

* Half of major properties have asset-level decarbonization roadmaps to reach net zero by 2040.

Original Press Release:

May 4 -- The Swig Company issued the following news release:

The Swig Company's latest ESG report, one of the few to be published annually by a private real estate investment firm in the United States, highlights its commitment to responsible ownership. By prioritizing stewardship of its assets, the firm achieved a 33% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions across its managed property portfolio since 2018, demonstrating that its ambition to reach net zero carbon by 2040 is well within reach.

The report also indicates that energy consumption throughout the portfolio fell by 27% last year compared to a 2018 baseline. Half of the company's major properties now have asset-level decarbonization roadmaps which will help guide asset and property management teams to reach net zero goals by 2040.

Announcing the release of The Swig Company's 2025 Environmental, Social and Governance Report, President and CEO Connor Kidd said: "Last year, our sustainability strategy moved from ambition to execution, a shift that is evident in the momentum we've achieved in reducing both GHG emissions and energy usage. Looking forward, we believe we are on the way to achieving a 50% reduction in GHG emissions by 2030 and net zero carbon by 2040, as we continue to pursue strategies across both our managed and non-managed portfolio."

In addition, over half of the company's non-managed portfolio-- comprised of assets in New York City-- reduced GHG emissions completely via Renewable Energy Certificates.

The firm's seventh annual report - covering environmental, social and governance (ESG) activities at 14 managed properties which provide space to 393 commercial tenants in California and Washington - reaffirms The Swig Company's commitment to environmental and economic sustainability, pursuing net zero, and fostering diversity, equity, and inclusion despite many companies shying away from these facets of industry.

"We're leaning in to ESG because we believe focusing on these issues strengthens our organization and, most importantly, supports resilient outcomes for our tenants, partners, and communities," said Kairee Tann, Senior Vice President, Director of Innovation and Community Impact.

Key takeaways from the 2025 report include:

Environmental:

* Decarbonization roadmaps completed for two properties, for a total of six.

*Transitioned the Mills Building in San Francisco to 100% renewable energy, bringing the total of buildings exclusively using renewables to five.

*2025 Performance:

*27% reduction in energy consumption from 2018, with 1% reduction from 2024 to 2025.

*33% reduction in GHG emissions intensity from 2018, with 7% reduction from 2024 to 2025.

*12% reduction in water consumption from 2018, with 3% reduction from 2024 to 2025.

Social:

*The Swig Company is an active, supportive neighbor in all of the communities where we invest. In 2025, we matched employee and board member donations to a wide range of causes, and supported our employees in volunteering for their favored causes.

*We continued providing on-the-job experience for high school YouthBeat apprentices through photography contracts, hosted 21 students of College Track at our San Francisco headquarters, held a College Track career panel in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of Los Angeles, welcomed our first College Track intern, and hosted spring and winter markets at the Mills Building and 350 California to support budding chefs at Life Learning Academy.

Governance:

*In 2025, we stayed informed on regulatory issues in San Francisco and California and successfully complied with the Washington State Clean Building Performance ordinance.

The ESG report is based on the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI), an industry standard framework, and our goals align with the Paris Agreement to limit global warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius.

To read the full report, visit: https://swigco.com/sustainability

About The Swig Company:

The Swig Company, LLC is a privately owned, San Francisco-based real estate operator with a 90-year history of investment, development, partnership and management of commercial real estate properties in major U.S. markets. Founded by Benjamin H. Swig in 1936, The Swig Company is guided by a long-term investment perspective that has proven adept at stewarding investors safely through multiple market cycles. The company's vertically integrated operational platform, which includes investment, asset and property management, and leasing expertise, uses flexibility, generational thinking and sensitivity to sustainable practices to stay at the forefront of emerging trends in the commercial real estate industry. For more information, visit: www.swigco.com.

[Category: Real Estate, Sustainability Initiatives, ESG and Sustainability, CSR]

Source: The Swig Company

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-VC63-RX36-R3MH-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS-LED FINANCING INJECTS OVER USD 180 MILLION INTO PURE SALMON JAPAN’S LAND-BASED SALMON PROJECT

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is earmarked to support the construction of Pure Salmon Japan’s land-based farming facility and to help meet rising domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores institutional backing for the project, which envisions a facility capable of producing up to 10,000 tonnes and potentially becoming Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. It relies on advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to bolster food security by reducing import dependency.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding presence in Japan, brings capital and expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, described the initiative as a meaningful step for strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS SENIOR HOUSING PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. The deal, completed on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by Fortress affiliates, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018 on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, the property serves a growing, affluent senior population along Florida's northeast coast. Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP ARRANGES $96 MILLION FINANCING FOR JASPER DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES MULTIFAMILY PROJECT

Fortress Investment Group has closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a newly developed 296-unit Class A multifamily community in Downtown Los Angeles. The financing, facilitated by Fortress affiliates, was completed on Feb. 9, 2026, highlighting Fortress's ongoing commitment to supporting high-quality real estate developments.Jasper is owned and developed by Cityview and sits near the University of Southern California campus. The property comprises studios, one- and two-bedroom residences featuring upscale finishes and a comprehensive amenity package, including affordable housing units. Proceeds from the loan will refinance existing construction debt and back the ongoing leasing efforts for the new community.Cityview's Damian Gancman described Fortress's involvement as efficient and solution-oriented, noting a seamless execution from start to finish. Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt at Fortress, said the project aligns with the firm's focus on backing high-quality, newly built multifamily housing with significant sponsor equity. Fortress, founded in 1998, manages about $54 billion in assets as of Sept. 30, 2025, and continues to invest across credit, real estate, private equity and permanent capital strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) PARTNERS WITH ALTERRA TO ACCELERATE GLOBAL CLIMATE INVESTMENTS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Partners With ALTERRA to Accelerate Global Climate InvestmentsKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) secured a commitment from the UAE's ALTERRA, a $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to bolster its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made Apr. 22, 2026, as KKR seeks to mobilize private capital for sustainable energy projects. The terms of the commitment were not disclosed.ALTERRA, launched at COP28 with UAE backing, aims to de-risk climate investments and has already formed partnerships with BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG. With KKR’s inclusion, ALTERRA now counts a broad set of investment expertise and targets up to $250 billion in mobilized climate finance by 2030, a substantial share of which is expected to flow to developing markets.KKR’s Global Climate Transition Strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, spans renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. The firm manages more than $100 billion in assets and has invested over $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable bets include Zenobe in the United Kingdom, EGC in Germany, Avantus in the United States and IGNIS P2X for industrial decarbonization. Gailliot and Lagarrigue highlighted that ALTERRA’s global reach combined with KKR’s infrastructure expertise could scale proven technologies and build more resilient energy systems.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) INVESTS IN STARITAS THROUGH ACCEL-KKR SPIN-OUT

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through its Accel-KKR unit to back the spin-out from ECR as it seeks growth in digital technology markets. The announcement signals KKR's continued focus on technology investments through Accel-KKR and aligns with Staritas' standalone growth plan. Staritas will operate independently from ECR to accelerate product development and market penetration, aided by the resources and expertise of Accel-KKR and KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR). The collaboration aims to position Staritas to respond quickly to evolving customer demands in the digital space. Investors will watch how the Staritas partnership unfolds in a competitive tech landscape, as the management team plans to expand offerings and strengthen service capabilities with this new capital and guidance.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT GLOBAL HEAD OF DIRECT LENDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut Global Head of Direct LendingTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending, effective Apr. 20, 2026. He has led KKR Real Estate's credit business, growing the platform from a seed investment of $400 million in 2015 to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by the end of 2025, and will oversee growth across a broader geographic footprint.Paul Fine, a 2015 hire, is named head of originations to succeed Traut. Fine, previously a managing director, will focus on strengthening client relationships and expanding the firm's capital base. The real estate credit team has grown from 10 employees in 2015 to 130 today, with Ali Imraan leading a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.The leadership transition signals a continued focus on growth and innovation in global real estate credit as KKR looks to capture opportunities across the U.S. and Europe.

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ELEVATES THREE EXECUTIVES TO CO-PRESIDENTS TO STRENGTHEN LEADERSHIP

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective from 22nd April 2026. The move aims to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as Carlyle refines its strategy in a challenging economic environment.The three co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and operational management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s franchises, positioning the firm to respond more quickly to market dynamics. By concentrating authority around Schwartz, Carlyle Group seeks greater execution discipline and improved performance in the coming quarters.

+ CARLYLE TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL FOR $2.8 BILLION, EXPANDING WEALTH-MANAGEMENT PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. confirmed on 31st March 2026 that it will acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The deal will transfer majority ownership to Carlyle while MAI employees retain a meaningful minority stake and the firm will continue to operate independently under Rick Buoncore, the chairman and chief executive.The transaction is designed to strengthen MAI's capital base and support growth in its financial planning, investment management and family-office capabilities. As of Jan 1, MAI and its affiliates oversee $72.6 billion in assets across 40 offices in the United States, a scale Carlyle will leverage with its global resources to maintain continuity for clients and advisors.The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary regulatory approvals. Carlyle has a history of originating, structuring and leading equity investments in wealth-management platforms. + CARLYLE TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL IN $2.8 BILLION DEAL

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has signed an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing the registered investment adviser at more than $2.8 billion. Under the terms, Carlyle will assume majority ownership, while existing stakeholders including Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital will exit; MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake. Carlyle previously invested in MAI in 2021 through Galway Holdings, which acquired the firm that year. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI manages $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 offices across the United States and has a workforce of more than 700. Despite volatility in Carlyle's stock, the parent firm maintains solid fundamentals, with liquid assets exceeding its near-term obligations and a 15-year streak of dividend payments, currently yielding around 3%. Financial analyses rate Carlyle's overall health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, Chairman and CEO of MAI, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, which facilitated MAI's growth through more than 30 strategic acquisitions during its ownership, said, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK THROUGH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks a milestone in the company's approach to managing its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026 and includes both amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and it introduced provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, granting lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, underscoring the company's financing approach. In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel committed to filing registration statements with the SEC to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This step aims to support liquidity and transparency for investors, with lenders indicating they will hold the securities for investment purposes only. The arrangements could lead to dilution if warrants are exercised and may increase the share count once registered for resale. BioXcel notes it is not an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting requirements; the registration rights agreement, involving several Oaktree entities among other holders, signals continued adherence to securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor forthcoming filings and announcements, as these developments could influence BioXcel's financial position and market performance.

+ OAKTREE AND NORTH MILL CLOSE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization with North Mill Equipment Finance, known as OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026. The deal underscores investor appetite for high-quality equipment finance assets.The securitization pools assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal finalized in November 2025. The offering was rated from AAA to single A and drew peak subscriptions of about 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack with a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%. Truist Securities acted as lead structuring agent and Deutsche Bank served as co-manager.

+ OAKTREE TO HONOR ALL REDEMPTIONS ON ABOUT $7.7 BILLION PRIVATE CREDIT FUND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would honor all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion. Investors will be allowed to redeem up to 8.5% of net assets, or roughly $400 million, with Brookfield contributing about $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund, which targets retail investors, is set to repurchase 6.8% of its own shares, with Brookfield agreeing to buy an additional 1.7% from a single investor, according to a letter to fund holders. The firm is also reducing its quarterly dividend by two cents, bringing the total to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, the fund reported $1.8 billion in liquidity from cash and undrawn facilities. Oaktree has also been selling portions of publicly traded loans and bonds this year to mitigate exposure to software investments, reflecting a broader portfolio-management push during a volatile period. The deal comes amid rising scrutiny of private credit markets, where peers such as Blackstone have faced higher-than-usual repurchase requests and have had to adjust buyback terms. Blackstone's $82 billion private credit fund faced repurchase requests exceeding the typical 5% threshold, prompting the board to raise the buyback to 7%.

The funding values Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC at a post-money valuation of approximately $9.62 billion and aims to strengthen operations and expand market presence. Apollo Global Management, Inc., via Apollo Funds, brings strategic backing to the company as demand for surgical solutions grows, with analysts watching how the partnership could influence growth and shareholder value as the transaction proceeds through regulatory review

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) INVESTS $1.25 BILLION IN MCKESSON CORPORATION (NYSE:MCK) FOR 13% STAKE IN MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS DIVISION

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The investment supports McKesson's plan to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division and is expected to bolster operational efficiencies across the group. McKesson intends to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division, and the capital from Apollo is expected to bolster the strategic plan and fund initiatives across the business. + APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT EXPANDS UK PRESENCE WITH GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP ACQUISITION

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a deal completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed. The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans. GLG is a national leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired over 5,000 homes. Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios, enabling GLG to leverage both Apollo capital and third-party partners to accelerate development of high-quality rental housing. Gatehouse Bank's divestiture will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN US$1.7 BILLION TRANSACTION

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding the firm’s footprint in necessity-based retail centers across rapidly growing metropolitan areas in the United States. The deal supports Ares' strategy to diversify across geographies and asset types and align with its emphasis on private credit, real assets, and special situations. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares Management named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring the firm’s focus on strengthening credit and special situations capabilities in Asia. The Whitestone purchase broadens Ares’ exposure to essential retail real estate and supports its strategy to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types. Investors should heed risks including profit margin compression, high debt levels, and a dividend yield of 5.38% that is not fully supported by earnings

+ ARES MANAGEMENT TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN $1.7 BILLION ALL-CASH DEAL

The announcement, made on Apr. 10, 2026, confirms that certain Ares Real Estate funds will acquire all common shares and operating partnership units of Whitestone REIT in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. Whitestone’s portfolio comprises 56 convenience-focused retail properties totaling about 4.9 million square feet in Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. After the deal closes, Whitestone will be private and will cease trading on the NYSE. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman said, "We believe Whitestone has shown the value of high-return smaller spaces occupied by a well-diversified mix of tenants. Our investment strategy is designed to allow businesses to fuel connection and convenience within thriving, dynamic communities. We believe this transaction with Ares is a testament to the value that strategy has created for our business and, ultimately, for our shareholders." David Roth, global head of real estate strategy and growth at Ares Real Estate, added, "This transaction reflects our high conviction in New Economy real estate as today’s consumers are increasingly seeking convenient experiences for their grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness, and dining needs." Ares Management said it expects to support and expand Whitestone's portfolio to create value for communities and investors.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT RAISES $5.4 BILLION FOR VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE RECOVERY

Ares Management Corporation (NYSE:ARES) has raised approximately $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in the United States and Europe, as of 9th April 2026. The fundraising underscores the firm's focus on opportunities in supply-constrained markets, with emphasis on logistics, multifamily, and self-storage assets that could rebound in the near term.The $5.4 billion package comprises about $1.9 billion for the EPEP IV fund and roughly $3.5 billion aimed at Ares' U.S. value-add strategy. Both lines have already deployed or identified around $1.1 billion in initial investments, signaling a proactive stance toward potential growth sectors.Analysts are broadly constructive, with about 75% of coverage bullish and a consensus price target near $161, implying a potential upside of roughly 60%. In a related note, on 24th March 2026, BMO Capital trimmed its price target for Ares from $140 to $112 and maintained a Market Perform rating amid concerns about BDC redemptions, asset-based credit issues, and market volatility. Ares, founded as a global alternative investment manager, also counts BlackRock among its significant backers, with a stake of about 7.12% as of the end of 2025. While Ares shows promise, some investors may find greater upside in certain AI stocks and other sectors in a rapidly shifting market.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESUMES UAE FREIGHT OPERATIONS FOLLOWING SAFETY REVIEW

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed its freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after a temporary halt due to safety concerns. As of 28th April 2026, the carrier published its new schedule, which includes the resumption of freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly, starting from 26th April. In addition, freighter services to Sharjah (SHJ) will operate three times weekly beginning on 1st May, while daily passenger flights to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with plans to expand freighter destinations to more than 50 by 1st May. The passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June.Shipment restrictions have also been lifted on certain urgent freight services to routes such as Victoria, Australia, with Qatar Airways Cargo offering products under new handling codes starting 1st May. The QRLIV codes cover commodities such as fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, while LHO codes cover the transport of live human organs and blood.Reservations remain accessible through the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, in addition to the standard sales channels. The airline says it remains committed to maintaining robust service levels as it ramps back up its operations.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS REVEALS NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE TO ELEVATE BUSINESS CLASS

Qatar Airways has announced the upcoming Qsuite Next Generation, slated to launch later this year after delays in its Boeing 777-9 arrivals. The upgraded business-class product will first appear on the Airbus A350-1000, as of 23rd April 2026. Building on the long-running Qsuite introduced nearly a decade ago, the Next Generation preserves sliding doors, double beds, and configurable seating while integrating advanced technology and improved space management to appeal to business travelers seeking privacy and flexibility. The move underscores Qatar Airways' strategy to lead premium travel, leveraging Doha Hamad International Airport's reputation and its premium ecosystem, including dedicated check-in, fast-track security, and lounge access.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS NETWORK AS RESTORATIONS CONTINUE

Qatar Airways has been expanding its operations, with belly and freighter services increasing to and from Doha. As of April 20, 2026, the carrier has restored its passenger network to more than 150 destinations and expanded freighter coverage to over 50 locations worldwide.The network restoration is part of a broader plan to rebuild capacity in the air cargo market. While the network grows, some shipments remain embargoed; AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes are restricted, and live organs and blood are not accepted. Customers can still book QRLIV shipments under alternative handling codes, provided the Doha transit time does not exceed 24 hours. All other products remain bookable through standard channels, including the Digital Lounge and local sales teams, underscoring the airline's adaptability to rising demand and shifting logistics conditions.
