+ CRAVATH PARTNER LAUNCHES BOUTIQUE DEDICATED TO CORPORATE INVESTIGATIONS

A Cravath Swaine & Moore partner has launched a boutique law firm dedicated to corporate investigations, signaling a shift toward specialized counsel as companies navigate increasingly complex regulatory demands. The new practice will offer internal investigations, compliance assessments, and risk management guidance, leveraging the founder’s Cravath experience to tailor services to clients under scrutiny.On 9th March 2026, the launch underscored a wider trend of seasoned practitioners leaving traditional firm structures to build agile, focused practices. The boutique aims to serve clients facing intricate corporate reviews with a high-touch, confidentiality-forward approach that larger firms often struggle to match.Observers anticipate a diverse roster, including Fortune 500 companies, private equity firms, and startups. The founder’s network and Cravath pedigree are expected to lend immediate credibility, while the emphasis on discretion and bespoke handling aims to differentiate the firm in a crowded market.

+ BIOCON SUCCEEDS $1.2 BILLION BIOSIMILARS DEAL, ENGAGING MULTIPLE LAW FIRMS

Biocon, the Indian biopharmaceutical company, has closed a $1.2 billion deal to fully consolidate its biosimilars operations by acquiring the remaining stakes from minority partners. The transaction, disclosed on December 11, 2025, is designed to streamline Biocon's footprint in a fast expanding biosimilars market and strengthen its growth trajectory.Viatris agreed to divest its remaining stake for about $815 million as part of the package, while other sellers include True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II. Serum Institute Life Sciences was among the sellers, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas advising it. Khaitan & Co and Cravath, Swaine & Moore advised Viatris on financing matters, and Veritas Legal provided counsel to True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II.Biocon tapped Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas to lead its advisory team, with Raghubir Menon and Natashaa Shroff at the helm. The firm also included Tarumoy Chaudhuri and Kriti Saraswat. The deal aligns with Biocon's ongoing integration with Viatris and its debt refinancing plans, underscoring the company's push to consolidate its biosimilars business amid a competitive market.

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE ANNOUNCES YEAR-END BONUSES, ASSOCIATES ELIGIBLE FOR UP TO $140,000

Cravath Swaine & Moore, one of the largest U.S. law firms, has officially launched the year-end bonus season by announcing that its associates will receive payouts of up to $140,000. On 20th November 2025, the New York-based firm communicated to its associates via an internal memo that annual bonuses will range from $15,000 for first-year associates to $115,000 for the most senior associates, according to information reported by Above the Law.In addition to the standard bonuses, Cravath plans to offer special bonuses ranging from $6,000 to $25,000, mirroring the payouts recently made by rival firm Milbank. This strategic move reinforces Cravath’s position as a traditional first mover in the competitive landscape of associate compensation within the legal sector. While the firm declined to provide further comments, the bonus structure aligns with expectations set by previous years.Following Cravath's announcement, other major firms are already responding. Paul Hastings, for instance, quickly disclosed its own year-end and special bonuses, which reflect a similar scale to those set by Cravath. A spokesperson from Paul Hastings also chose not to comment, indicating that the firm is likely following the trend established by Cravath.The bonuses announced by Cravath Swaine & Moore are consistent with those distributed by large U.S. law firms last year, as many associates now earn annual salaries ranging from $225,000 to $435,000 depending on their class year. The competitive nature of the legal profession often leads firms to match salary and bonus figures to retain top talent, and Cravath’s decision is expected to influence other firms in the sector.Looking ahead, U.S. law firms are poised for a profitable 2025, with large and midsized firms experiencing a significant increase in client demand, particularly in transactional practices. This positive outlook was highlighted in a recent analysis of firm financial data, suggesting that the legal industry is gearing up for another successful year, further emphasizing the importance of competitive compensation packages like those offered by Cravath.

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has completed its Hong Kong IPO, raising HK$4.4 billion (about $562 million). + DAVIS POLK EXPANDS WEST COAST FOOTPRINT WITH LOS ANGELES OFFICE OPENING

Davis Polk & Wardwell said on 14th April 2026 that it will establish a Los Angeles office, appointing Jason Russell to lead the expansion. The move underscores the firm's bid to grow its West Coast footprint.Russell, a veteran litigator who previously ran Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom's Los Angeles office and served on its policy committee, brings a portfolio of high-profile clients including the NBA, Christie’s and MGA Entertainment that the firm hopes to leverage in California.Davis Polk plans to hire additional lawyers for the new LA office and to accelerate its local capabilities, signaling an intent to compete with other leading California firms and win significant matters as the market heats up.

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund, a milestone that underscores investor confidence in its strategy. urn:contentItem:6JGB-F2B3-S5GJ-81VG-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: May 01, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Celebrates 60 Years in Nairobi , Signals Africa-Centered Development Push

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-CXJ3-RTBH-G0NJ-00000-00May 01, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Celebrates 60 Years in Nairobi, Signals Africa-Centered Development Push2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 01, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Celebrates 60 Years in Nairobi, Signals Africa-Centered Development Push

NAIROBI — The Rockefeller Foundation marked the 60th anniversary of its Africa Regional Office, established in Nairobi, Kenya, in 1966, on the 1st of May, 2026. The milestone was celebrated as part of AfricaXchange 2026, a forum to catalyze systemic reform by uniting leaders who can mobilize capital, influence policy discussions, and scale viable solutions to Africa’s challenges. The anniversary event drew more than 400 leaders from business, government, and civil society, underscoring the Foundation’s century-long work in human capital, health, agriculture, and energy across the continent.

William Asiko, Senior Vice President and head of the Africa Regional Office, noted the changing landscape of development finance: "African philanthropy, private capital, and regional institutions are reshaping not just how development is financed, but how it is delivered." His remarks came as OECD data showed a decline in official development assistance, reinforcing the need for new investment models and stronger partnerships that position Africa as an equal partner in shaping its economic future.

In the closing session, Elizabeth Yee, Executive Vice President of Programs, insisted that philanthropy’s job is not to be the hero: "It’s to take risk others can’t, build trust where it’s been missing, and move countries from ambition to execution." The conference theme, "Money, Markets, and Mindsets," reflected the aim of financing and leading Africa with African voices. The Rockefeller Foundation also launched the inaugural Africa Big Bets Fellows, a class of ten changemakers advancing community-led solutions across several countries. The event highlighted cross-sector collaboration through participants such as E. Prof. Anyang’ Nyong’o, Governor of Kisumu County, and E. Nardos Bekele-Thomas, CEO of the African Union Development Agency.

The Foundation’s long relationship with Africa predates the Nairobi office, tracing back to early 20th-century initiatives including the 1925 West Africa Yellow Fever Commission. Today, roughly one-third of The Rockefeller Foundation’s global funding supports Africa, with priorities spanning energy abundance, food security, health, and innovation. By emphasizing local solutions and strengthened partnerships, the Rockefeller Foundation aims to advance a sustainable, prosperous future for the continent.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PROFILE

SECTION 2 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

value.Title: April 23, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Commits to Workforce Resilience Amid AI Disruption

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-KN63-RX1M-C3Y7-00000-00April 23, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Commits to Workforce Resilience Amid AI Disruption2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 23, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Commits to Workforce Resilience Amid AI Disruption

On 28th April 2026, the Rockefeller Foundation announced a grant-based national initiative to address disruptions from automation, aiming to prevent job displacement and help workers transition into AI-complementary roles. The program prioritizes workforce interventions over product development, stressing program design, metrics and evaluation. It will measure displacement risk, deployment outcomes and labor-market transitions to understand how automation affects employment. Funding will come through grants, programmatic pilots and partnerships with employers, training providers and local governments. The choices made during implementation will be critical in determining whether the foundation's funding yields scalable reskilling pipelines or merely funds isolated projects. This initiative is a strategic philanthropic signal rather than a regulatory or corporate move, intended to catalyze innovative retraining models and public-private collaboration, complementing ongoing discussions about workforce policies such as sectoral training and apprenticeships. It is also expected to boost demand for measurable, data-driven evaluations within the skills and labor analytics ecosystem.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGG-3XS3-S7C2-R1FN-00000-00MILBANK LLP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKMILBANK LLP - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK PROPOSES PHASED, LONG-TERM PROTECTION FOR ASH CANOPY

Milbank is deploying a strategic plan to combat the emerald ash borer, aiming to protect its ash trees from nearly half of its canopy. The infestation, detected last summer, threatens trees in parks and the cemetery, with removal costs projected up to $4 million.The city’s alternative is a 'herd immunity' program, estimated at $2.4 million over 12 years, centered on targeted treatments and ongoing monitoring. Officials say the approach seeks to preserve the urban forest while reducing beetle spread.The plan relies on cooperation among local leaders, environmental experts, and residents, with a phased rollout prioritizing vulnerable areas. Milbank hopes the initiative may offer a replicable model for other communities facing the same pest challenge.

+ MILBANK LAWYERS EXPLAIN CFTC'S NEW GUIDANCE ON PREDICTION MARKETS AND ITS LEGAL RAMIFICATIONS

Milbank analyzed the CFTC’s new guidance on prediction markets in a recent webinar, which followed the agency’s 17th March 2026 announcement introducing a regulatory expectations advisory and an Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking with a 45-day public comment period ending 30th April.Partner Josh Sterling highlighted the rapid growth of prediction markets, rising from $3 billion in trading volume in January 2025 to $27 billion in January 2026, drawing attention from market participants, courts, and state regulators. The event also featured Milbank litigation partners Grant Mainland, Matt Laroche, and John Williams.Laroche said the advisory signals the CFTC’s belief that sports-event contracts fall within its jurisdiction, a notable clarification. He added that the advisory acknowledges prediction markets as a distinct financial asset class that is expanding and can provide useful information. Williams pointed to the rulemaking notice as a strong stance by the CFTC to claim jurisdiction, while leaving future steps less defined, noting the questions for public comment on hedging, price risk, and information dissemination.Milbank suggested practical steps for operators, including reviewing listings for compliance, engaging with leagues, mitigating manipulation risks, participating in rulemaking, and watching blockchain developments. Mainland also noted more than 20 active court cases involving prediction markets, illustrating a legal environment that remains complex and unsettled, especially regarding whether the Commodity Exchange Act preempts state law.

The case traces back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and expropriation of 51 percent of YPF. + FROM REGULATION TO CRYPTO: RODGE COHEN’S LONG VIEW ON BANKING’S FUTURE

H. Rodgin "Rodge" Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has helped shape banking policy for more than five decades. On 19th February 2026, at an NYU Law Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance event, he reflected on the evolution of the financial system, with remarks from Jay Clayton and Edward Rock providing context on today’s regulatory landscape.Cohen cited landmark regulatory shifts, including the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, which broadened access to banking and redefined compliance, contributing to a drop in the unbanked rate from roughly one in three to five percent. Clayton noted the regulation changes since the 1970s, while Cohen’s advocacy supported interstate banking moves in the 1980s that reshaped the industry’s geography.He recalled the 2008 crisis, representing major banks as policymakers acted to stabilize markets. Cohen described the tense hours before the government’s intervention after Lehman Brothers’ collapse, saying, "We were on the edge of a cliff," a moment that underscored the urgency of decisive action and cemented his standing as a premier financial-services lawyer.Looking ahead, Cohen warned that cryptocurrency regulation must keep pace with rapid market evolution. He emphasized that regulators must avoid missteps, citing Gresham’s Law that "bad regulation drives out good regulation" and underscoring Sullivan & Cromwell’s ongoing role in guiding the sector’s future.

urn:contentItem:6JK3-WT63-RSWV-81GY-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH PERKINS COIE AND OTHERS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH PERKINS COIE AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ ADDING TO GLOBAL TOP FOUR EMERGING COMPANIES & VENTURE CAPITAL TEAM, PILLSBURY WELCOMES PARTNER JOHN ROCKWELL IN SILICON VALLEY

Continuing the expansion of Pillsbury's global Emerging Companies & Venture Capital practice, the firm has welcomed another well-known partner to the team: John Rockwell in Silicon Valley. Rockwell, who comes to Pillsbury from WilmerHale, brings valuable cross-border experience in Latin America and even greater depth to a practice that was recently ranked by LSEG as among the four most active in the world for the representation of company-side venture capital financings.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY PREVAILS IN WINERY DISPUTE AFTER FIVE-DAY JURY TRIAL

Ateam of Pillsbury lawyers led by Austin-based Litigation partner Casey Low prevailed in a five-day jury trial in favor of client Fred Schrader, the founder of the highly acclaimed Schrader Cellars winery, and the winery's current owner.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ KING & SPALDING STEPHEN VAUGHN NAMED ONE OF D.C.S 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE BY WASHINGTONIAN MAGAZINE

King &Spalding Partner Stephen Vaughn has been named one of Washington, D.C.'s 500 Most Influential People by Washingtonian magazine.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS SEASONED FINANCE AND RESTRUCTURING SHAREHOLDER GERARD C. MARTIN IN CHICAGO

CHICAGO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added secured finance attorney Gerard C. Martin to its Chicago office as a shareholder in the firm's Restructuring & Special Situations Practice. He joins the firm from Reed Smith LLP.

Martin brings deep experience across the full spectrum of financing transactions and debt restructurings. He represents lenders, borrowers, sponsors, and investors in a broad range of U.S. and international financing transactions, including cash flow and asset-based loans, leveraged loans, acquisition financings, bridge facilities, recapitalizations, and complex intercreditor arrangements, including unitranche and multitranche structures. He regularly advises on large, syndicated credit facilities and cross-border and multicurrency financings. Martin also maintains an active equipment and aviation finance practice, representing parties in aircraft and engine financings, leveraged leases, rolling stock transactions, and project finance matters. He works with clients across a wide range of industries, including manufacturing, energy, aviation, technology, health care, and financial services, among others.

(the "Company"), the master franchisee of Burger King and Popeyes in Poland, Czechia and Romania, as well as Rex Invest CEE - a company in the McWin group - as the selling shareholder, on the initial public offering (IPO) of the Company's shares and the admission of the shares to trading on the regulated market operated by the Warsaw Stock Exchange.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISES SELLER ON HYPERSCALE DATA CENTER TRANSACTION IN JAPAN

TOKYO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented the seller in the disposition of a stabilized hyperscale data center located in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The transaction is among the largest asset‑level sales of a fully leased, operational hyperscale data center in the Japanese market to date.

The approximately 40‑megawatt facility is leased to a single U.S. hyperscale cloud provider. The seller is a joint venture between a fund sponsored by a U.S. institutional manager and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company, and the buyer is a consortium comprising Singapore‑listed CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, which acquired a 49% interest, and a fund managed by an affiliate of the Japanese trading company, which acquired the remaining 51%.

urn:contentItem:6JK4-M4G3-RRYS-M1G0-00000-00Michigan House advances GOP energy reform package2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:50ZLexisNexisRENEWABLE ENERGY NEWS, Coal industry news, US news, US politics news & US STATE GOVERNMENT NEWSMichigan House advances GOP energy reform packageBy Elyse Apel | The Center Square

(The Center Square) - Michigan House Republicans successfully advanced a major overhaul of the state's energy policies they say will lower utility bills and improve grid reliability.

The Republican-led legislation, House Bills 5710-5711, passed the House this week and now heads to the Democrat-held Senate.

State Rep. Ann Bollin, R-Brighton Township, said Michigan residents are struggling under rising utility bills and this package brings immediate relief.

"Families are telling me they are doing everything right and still falling behind because of higher utility costs," Bollin explained following the vote. "When electric bills keep climbing month after month, it creates real stress for parents, seniors living on fixed incomes, and hardworking people trying to make ends meet. Michigan families need some relief."

Bollin said the legislation would help ensure residents can rely on power during extreme weather while reducing costs.

"People should not have to worry about whether they can afford to keep the heat on in the winter or the air conditioning running during dangerous summer temperatures," Bollin said.

State Rep. Ron Robinson, R-Utica, described the package as a "top-to-bottom reset" of Michigan energy policy.

"We are righting the ship," Robinson said. "This is a serious, top-to-bottom reset of Michigan's energy policy that puts affordability and reliability first for families, workers, and job providers."

The bills would:

· Requires the Michigan Public Service Commission to prioritize affordability and reliability

· Stops funding to outside political activist groups

· Streamlines the process for building and upgrading energy infrastructure

· Increases transparency around ratehikes

These bills come just a couple years after Gov. Gretchen Whitmer joined fellow Democrats to pass a series of public acts cementing the state's clean energy strategy.

Among other provisions, the legislation established a new statewide standard requiring utilities to generate 80% of their electricity from clean sources by 2035 and reach 100% clean energy by 2040.

This new legislation is part of a broader House Republican affordability agenda that lawmakers say could save Michigan families roughly $1,400 annually through a combination of utility and property tax relief.

Environmental groups and clean energy advocates sharply criticized the proposals, arguing the bills would eliminate some of the state's most effective cost-saving energy policies.

Ben Poulson, state government affairs director for the Michigan League of Conservation Voters, told The Center Square that the reforms would make it harder to expand low-cost renewable energy and energy efficiency programs.

"It's disappointing that at a time when Michiganders are already struggling with rising energy costs, the House has chosen to pass reforms that make it harder to deploy some of the cheapest and cleanest energy resources available to Michigan families and businesses," Poulson said. "These proposals move us in the wrong direction at a moment when people are looking for real solutions to lower their bills and strengthen our energy future."

Poulson pointed to estimates that project billions in future savings if the current energy programs stay in place.

"Repealing clean energy targets will only make matters worse, as research shows it could instead lead to $5.6 billion in higher electricity costs statewide between 2026 and 2050," Poulson said. "At a time when energy costs are already rising, rolling back some of the most effective tools we have to reduce energy bills is deeply concerning."

He also argued the legislation would create additional barriers for homeowners seeking to install rooftop solar or other distributed energy systems.

"This legislation also makes it more difficult for individuals to take control of their own energy future," Poulson said. "By weakening requirements around connecting distributed energy resources like rooftop solar, it creates additional barriers for Michiganders who want to invest in their homes, lower their bills, and contribute to a more resilient energy system."

Supporters of the legislation reject claims the reforms would raise costs.

David Stevenson, director of energy and environmental policy at the Mackinac Center for Public Policy, told The Center Square that Michigan's current clean energy mandates are driving up utility bills by pushing utilities toward intermittent power sources.

"The reforms are exactly what Michigan needs to reduce energy costs and ensure reliability by allowing dependable energy plants to operate while removing mandates that favor intermittent, costly energy sources like wind and solar," Stevenson said.

Stevenson cited a Mackinac Center study projecting utility bills could rise significantly if Michigan continues transitioning away from coal and nuclear power.

"A Mackinac Center study projects that transitioning from dependable resources like coal and nuclear to solar and wind could dramatically increase ratepayers utility bills by more than $2,000 a year in the coming years," Stevenson said.

He added that renewable energy sources currently provide a relatively small share of Michigan's electricity generation and argued "dependable resources like coal and nuclear" remain necessary during periods of high demand.

"Wind and solar energy don't meet Michigan's energy needs in all seasons," Stevenson said. "Today, solar power provides under 3% of Michigan's electricity and wind under 8%, and those limited contributions show why dependable energy is essential. Dependable energy sources remain essential for affordability and reliability."

Both bills passed with only Republican support in the House.

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Pillsbury Announces Boston Office as Part of Global Expansion

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK2-7YM3-RS4R-20GJ-00000-00May 05, 2026: Pillsbury Announces Boston Office as Part of Global Expansion2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:14ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Pillsbury Announces Boston Office as Part of Global Expansion

On 12th May 2026, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman announced plans to open an office in Boston, expanding its presence in a major U.S. legal market with a team of five senior lawyers, including a litigator hired from Latham & Watkins.

Robert Gilbert, who arrives from Latham after seven years, focuses on policyholder insurance recovery and brings experience with Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. He will help establish the Boston office alongside Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.

Chair and CEO David Dekker said the Boston outpost aligns with the city’s startup and research strengths, noting Pillsbury’s long history of serving Boston-area clients. He added, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world." This expansion marks Pillsbury’s 17th U.S. location and 25th globally, with Boston joining a wave of firms increasing their Boston presence.

The move underscores Pillsbury’s intent to deepen ties with local clients and industries while leveraging its global platform in technology, life sciences, healthcare, and finance.

INDEX

SECTION 1 PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP PROFILE

SECTION 2 PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

Mission, Vision, and Values

At Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, our mission is clear: to deliver innovative, high-quality legal services while positively impacting the communities we serve. Since our founding in 1868, we've built a reputation as a leading global law firm, combining forward-thinking legal strategies with a commitment to excellence.

Vision

Our vision is to be recognized as a premier global law firm, distinguished by our exceptional legal representation and unwavering dedication to public service.

Core Values

Innovation

We continuously innovate and refine our legal services to stay at the forefront of the industry.

Client Focus

Our priority is always our clients-anticipating their needs and delivering unmatched service.

Diversity and Inclusion

We are deeply committed to fostering a culture of diversity and equity, empowering our people to grow and succeed.

Community Impact

Beyond legal counsel, we strive to make a lasting difference through pro bono work, philanthropy, and volunteerism.

Integrity

We uphold the highest standards of transparency and honesty in every aspect of our practice, building trusted partnerships with clients and the global community.

With a presence in major cities such as New York, San Francisco, London, and Shanghai, Pillsbury merges a profound legacy with cutting-edge innovation to empower clients and drive progress worldwide.
