DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS LANDS PILLSBURY VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN EMPLOYEE BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team, a move designed to address increasing complexities in benefits law and the growing need for specialized guidance.The attorney brings broad experience in compliance and regulatory matters across employee benefits, and is expected to enhance Morgan Lewis' ability to provide comprehensive services to clients. Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire reinforces the firm's ongoing strategy to expand its practice areas as client needs evolve.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST-PERKINS COIE MERGER SECURES STRONG PARTNER BACKING

The merger of Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie has won overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. The decision was confirmed after a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The deal is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, subject to customary closing conditions.Perkins Coie's Mark Birnbaum said the approval reflects partners' commitment to creating a forward looking firm and a shared confidence in their ability to grow and deliver high quality legal services in a changing global market. Ashurst chief executive Paul Jenkins stressed the alignment between the two practices and that combining strengths will benefit clients.The merged firm will focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services. Perkins Coie firmwide managing partner Bill Malley expressed optimism that the combination will improve the firms capacity to handle complex cross border matters. The new entity will count roughly 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York.The merger first announced last year brings together two law firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have wide international experience, having worked in Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst global chair Karen Davies and Perkins Coie corporate practice chair Brian Eiting will serve as co-chairs.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST SEAL MERGER TO CREATE ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union with complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The new entity will count roughly 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, with flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. It will be noted for strengths in technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services sectors—industries pivotal to the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, said, 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform—one with the scale, sector depth, and technological leadership to meet our clients' increasingly cross-border needs.' Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, added, 'This vote confirms the strong alignment between our firms and our joint ambition for the future,' highlighting the complementary expertise across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. The merger will create a compelling platform for top legal talent.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026, positioning the new firm to navigate the complexities of the global market and deliver enhanced legal services.

+ PERKINS COIE OPENS SHENZHEN OFFICE, SIGNALING RETURN TO CHINA'S MARKET

Perkins Coie has opened a representative office in Shenzhen, marking its comeback in the Chinese market. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026, following the closure of its Beijing and Shanghai outposts in 2024 and a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to tap the city’s rapid growth as a technology and manufacturing hub. Leading the Shenzhen office are Bing Ai, chief representative and managing partner, and Wei Yuan, representative and litigation partner. Both are US-trained and bring substantial experience in advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, said the Shenzhen move reinforces the firm's commitment to providing US legal expertise across borders and helping clients navigate global markets amid rising cross-border challenges. The development also unfolds as several international firms push deeper into Shenzhen's Greater Bay Area, even as Perkins Coie weighs a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst that could create a larger global platform.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY SECURES $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING FROM COOLEY LLP AND LEADING FIRMS

Crosby, a contract review start-up, has raised $60 million in its latest Series B, marking the company’s third capital raise in under a year. The funding announcement was made on April 3, 2026, with Cooley LLP among the investors backing Crosby.The round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors and Bain Capital Ventures, along with angel investor Elad Gil. The fresh capital will support product development and scale Crosby’s platform to serve clients such as Clay, Rogo and Ramp, a financial services operations platform.Crosby fuses artificial intelligence with legal expertise to accelerate contract reviews. The company says the value of negotiated contracts has surpassed $1 billion, a marked jump from roughly $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby noted, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has rolled out simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate on behalf of clients, and a dashboard that lets clients oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and tech lead for Ramp.

+ COOLEY LLP EXPANDS TECHNOLOGY PRACTICE WITH FRESHFIELDS ALUMNI

Cooley LLP has announced a significant expansion of its technology practice, bringing on board several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. This strategic move, aimed at bolstering Cooley's capabilities in the tech sector, highlights the firm's commitment to remaining competitive in an increasingly dynamic legal landscape.As of 23rd November 2025, Cooley's latest recruitment drive underscores the firm's ongoing efforts to enhance its service offerings in technology, a sector that continues to evolve rapidly. The arrival of these high-profile Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to not only strengthen Cooley's existing practice but also attract new clients seeking expert legal guidance in technology-related matters.The new team members bring a wealth of experience, having advised prominent clients on a range of legal issues, including intellectual property, data protection, and corporate transactions in the tech industry. Their expertise is anticipated to complement Cooley’s established reputation in venture capital and startup advisory services, further positioning the firm as a leading player in the technology legal sector.Cooley LLP’s decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader trend within the legal industry, where firms are actively seeking to acquire top talent to respond to the demands of a fast-paced, technology-driven market. As legal challenges in the tech sector become more complex, the need for specialized knowledge and skills is paramount, making these strategic hires particularly timely.With this expansion, Cooley LLP aims to solidify its standing as a go-to destination for clients in the technology field, reinforcing its capabilities in an area that is critical for innovation and growth. As the firm integrates its new attorneys, stakeholders will be watching closely to see how these changes impact Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH ADDS PRIVATE EQUITY PARTNER IN LOS ANGELES TO EXPAND PRACTICE

ReedSmith LLP has added a new private equity partner in its Los Angeles office, reinforcing its presence in a key market for investments. The hire broadens the firm's private equity capabilities and aligns with its long-term strategy to serve investment firms and corporate clients involved in mergers and acquisitions.The partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings extensive experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services. The addition is designed to help Reed Smith navigate complex deals and deliver tailored legal solutions as demand in the region continues to rise.This appointment underscores Reed Smith's commitment to high-quality service and enduring client relationships as private equity activity grows on the West Coast.

+ REED SMITH LLP NAMES INTERNAL LEADER TO HEAD PHILADELPHIA OFFICE

ReedSmith LLP has announced the appointment of a seasoned attorney to lead its Philadelphia office, marking a significant transition for the firm in a key market. The chosen leader is a longtime member of the firm whose decade-long tenure includes involvement in high-profile matters and contributions to regional growth. Effective from 27th April 2026, the appointment underscores Reed Smith's commitment to strengthening its local presence and client service in Philadelphia.

+ REED SMITH APPOINTS ITS FIRST MANAGING PARTNER IN BOSTON TO LEAD REGIONAL EXPANSION

ReedSmith LLP has appointed its first Managing Partner in Boston, a strategic step to strengthen the firm's Northeast presence. Effective from 23rd April 2026, the move will place a local leader at the helm of Boston operations to deepen client relationships and drive business development in the market. The appointment follows an assessment of the area's growing demand for specialized legal services and Reed Smith's push to expand in key markets. The new leader will harness local insights and partnerships to align with the firm's global plan and to embed itself more deeply in Boston's legal ecosystem. With a robust network and extensive experience, the Managing Partner is expected to improve operational efficiency and boost the firm's standing as a top provider of legal services in the Northeast. The Boston office is anticipated to play a central role in Reed Smith's broader growth strategy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOORBIT SECURES $13.2M SEED ROUND; WILSON SONSINI ADVISES SPACE PHARMA STARTUP

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. advised BioOrbit on its $13.2 million seed financing, announced on 30th April 2026. The round was co-led by LocalGlobe and BREEGA, with participation from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, 7percent Ventures and other investors.BioOrbit, led by Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, is aiming to build a space-based pharmaceuticals factory within the next decade. The company is developing hardware to enable large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, a key step in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.Wilson Sonsini's team — Daniel Glazer, Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed and Sophia Kunthara — provided corporate and emerging-company counsel to BioOrbit throughout the financing, helping position the startup for its ambitious plans in space manufacturing.

+ WILSON SONSINI GUIDES OFF PISTE CAPITAL THROUGH INAUGURAL FUND LAUNCH

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. announced its advisory role in the launch of Off Piste Capital's inaugural fund. Off Piste Fund I closed on 29th April 2026 with backing from strategic limited partners including Edition Ventures.The fund targets companies building the systems and infrastructure of the future and has already invested in four startups: Stendr in Norway, Objection in the UK, Beyond Reach Labs in the US, and Octet in the UK.Wilson Sonsini's deal team including Bradley Doline, Alice Cao, and Stephanie Yasay guided fund formation and closing. The collaboration underscores the firm's commitment to supporting emerging venture activity, and with Fund I now live Off Piste plans to back more innovative solutions. Details are available via Off Piste's LinkedIn and EU Startups coverage.

+ WILSON SONSINI EXPANDS ENERGY AND CLIMATE SOLUTIONS TEAM WITH KEN SANOCKI

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati named Ken Sanocki a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, reinforcing the firm's capacity to advise on energy infrastructure financing amid the transition to cleaner power.Sanocki comes from Sunrun, where he served as vice president of legal and oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and complex debt structures for large distributed-energy projects. He led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling more than $10 billion.Doug Clark, Wilson Sonsini's managing partner, said, "Ken has operated at the center of the energy transition, helping shape the financing structures that have enabled distributed energy to scale into mainstream infrastructure." In his new role, Sanocki will counsel clients on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, and assist with capital formation, strategic transactions, and potential IPOs or M&A.Sanocki began his career at Milbank and most recently was senior counsel for global structured and project finance at SunEdison. He holds a J.D. from NYU School of Law and a B.A. from Boston College. His arrival, alongside recent partner additions, positions the firm to lead energy-transition finance and clean-energy advisory.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN ADVISES TELIX IN USD 2.1 BILLION REGENERON COLLABORATION

Sidley Austin has advised Telix Pharmaceuticals on its strategic collaboration with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion. The agreement will have Telix and Regeneron co-developing and co-commercialising next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies under a 50:50 cost- and profit-sharing model, with Telix receiving USD 40 million upfront for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the partnership emphasizes joint governance and shared development and commercialization responsibilities designed to leverage both companies' oncology platforms. The collaboration aims to speed the advancement of radiopharmaceuticals in cancer treatment by combining Regeneron's oncology capabilities with Telix's radiopharmaceutical pipeline. Joshua Hofheimer, a partner at Sidley Austin, remarked that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The deal underscores Telix's expansion strategy and reflects the growing role of law firms like Sidley Austin in guiding complex, cross-border biopharma collaborations as regulatory landscapes evolve.

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN REPORTS 33% CITY REVENUE INCREASE ON STRATEGIC HIRING

Sidley Austin has reported a 33% rise in City revenue, a result the firm attributes to its strategic hiring, with the trend noted as of 14th April 2026. Management says the recruitment of top-tier talent has broadened the firm's City teams, expanding service offerings and driving higher billable hours and client retention. Analysts say the move reflects a broader shift toward investing in human capital to stay competitive, and stakeholders will watch how the strategy translates into sustained performance.

+ CFTC LAUNCHES INNOVATION TASK FORCE WITH SIDLEY AUSTIN AND LATHAM TO DRIVE DERIVATIVES REGULATION

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has formed an Innovation Task Force by engaging Sidley Austin and Latham & Watkins to sharpen the regulatory framework governing the derivatives market, signaling a proactive embrace of technology and innovation in finance.In an announcement dated 11th April 2026, the commission said the task force will draw on the combined regulatory experience of Sidley and Latham to help shape policies that reflect current market realities, including digital assets, blockchain and AI. The goal is to balance effective oversight with practical pathways for innovation and to keep the United States a leader in global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG HELPS FACILITATE GOLDEN ENTERTAINMENT’S SHIFT TO PRIVATE FAMILY OWNERSHIP

Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented Blake L. Sartini in the successful transition of Golden Entertainment to a private, family-owned operation. The transaction was finalized on 30th April 2026, marking a milestone for Sartini, a prominent figure in the gaming sector.Under the deal, Golden Entertainment shareholders received a cash dividend of $2.75 and 0.902 shares of VICI Properties Inc. for each share of Golden stock. The arrangement is designed to provide immediate liquidity while preserving investors' stake in a major gaming and hospitality company.VICI Properties acquired the real estate assets of seven Golden casino resorts, while a triple-net master lease was established with entities controlled by Sartini, allowing Golden to continue operating its casino and tavern portfolio without disruption. Michael J. Bonner, Co-Managing Shareholder of Greenberg Traurig's Las Vegas office, commented on the milestone and expressed pride in representing Sartini. He said, "We were honored to represent Blake L. Sartini in returning Golden Entertainment to private family ownership. We congratulate Blake and the entire Golden Entertainment team!" The firm noted its depth in corporate transactions and real estate law, and highlighted its team members, including Dmitriy A. Tartakovskiy, Jim Mace, and James Doyle, among others.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO EXPLORES AI IMPACTS ON REAL ASSETS AND DATA CENTRES

Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non-Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing institutional investors and industry experts together to discuss the evolving landscape of real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence. The event took place on the 30th of April, 2026, and featured a panel titled Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities. Panelist Joel H. Rothstein, a Greenberg Traurig Shareholder who chairs the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, joined colleagues in examining how artificial intelligence could reshape capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a focus on the surge in demand for data centre assets across the Asia-Pacific region driven by digitalization. Rothstein described Greenberg Traurig's ongoing commitment to fostering industry dialogue around pivotal trends shaping real estate and infrastructure. He stated, 'These conversations are increasingly important as investors and developers navigate the rapid convergence of digital infrastructure, real estate, and emerging technologies.' The firm draws on its cross-border platform to support complex, multijurisdictional investments, and its global footprint includes about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations. Greenberg Traurig is recognized for its real estate and infrastructure work and has earned accolades such as the 2025 BTI Best of the Best Recommended Law Firm, along with listings in Am Law Global 100 and Law360 400.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG’S MELTONES WIN LAW ROCKS LA, DONATING TO VETERANS PROGRAMS

On 29th April 2026, Greenberg Traurig, LLP's house band The Meltones won Law Rocks Los Angeles at Whisky A Go Go in West Hollywood, driving more than $65,000 in donations and sponsorships, including $41,000 for The Headstrong Project, a nonprofit that provides mental health resources to military veterans.The three-night event set a new record, tallying more than $569,000 for Los Angeles charities—the highest total in the city in 15 years. The Meltones opened the show with a Green Day classic, and their victory was determined by a combination of donations, ticket sales, and judges' scores. Greenberg Traurig served as a Hall of Fame sponsor for the event.The band is part of the firm’s longstanding culture; The Meltones’ lineup includes Stephen L. Rabinowitz and Burt Bruton on guitar, Christopher A. Cernik on keyboard, and vocalists Michael R. Hogue, Ashley A. LeBlanc, and Tracy Newirth, with Tom Helf on drums and Daniel Blumberg on bass as guest artists. Named after co-founder Mel Greenberg, the group has helped define GT’s philanthropic spirit since the mid-1980s, a tradition reinforced by recent Law Rocks NYC success that raised over $100,000 for The Door. As Greenberg Traurig grows to about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations, social responsibility remains core to the firm’s identity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESPERION THERAPEUTICS TO GO PRIVATE IN $1.1 BILLION DEAL, WITH SIDLEY AND GOODWIN LEADING THE TRANSACTION

Esperion Therapeutics has agreed to a $1.1 billion take-private transaction, with Sidley Austin LLP and Goodwin Procter LLP leading the legal work. The deal, announced on 1st May 2026, aims to shift the lipid-management specialist away from public market volatility and toward focus on its core programs. The arrangement is expected to reshape Esperion's operating framework as it pivots toward private ownership.The move reflects a broader trend in biotech toward private ownership as a means to streamline research and development and accelerate commercialization. Sidley and Goodwin's involvement underscores the deal's complexity and the firms' strength in mergers and acquisitions as Esperion transitions to a private structure.

+ FBT GIBBONS EXPANDS VENTURE CAPITAL PRACTICE WITH HIRE FROM GOODWIN PROCTER

FBT Gibbons announced the addition of a venture capital attorney from Goodwin Procter LLP to its New Jersey office, with the hire confirmed on 21st April 2026. The move is intended to strengthen the firm's venture capital capabilities and better serve clients in a competitive market. The attorney brings extensive experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a recognized leader in venture capital work for startups and established companies alike, and FBT Gibbons plans to leverage that expertise to guide clients through fundraising and complex investment environments. This development aligns with a broader industry trend of law firms expanding niche practices in venture capital.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER GUIDES TOPSPIN TO $328 MILLION CONSUMER FUND CLOSE

Goodwin Procter LLP helped close Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising, securing $328 million for a new consumer-focused vehicle, a notable development in private equity. On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the close, noting that Goodwin Procter provided essential legal support throughout the process.The fund is designed to capitalize on opportunities across the consumer sector, with Topspin aiming to identify and back innovative companies positioned to thrive in a dynamic market. Goodwin Procter’s guidance on regulatory matters and partnership structures is expected to enhance operational efficiency and investor confidence.This collaboration underscores Goodwin Procter’s strength in private equity and signals a continuing trend toward consumer-focused investments as the market expands.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK BOLSTERS IP BENCH WITH SENIOR USPTO ATTORNEY HIRE

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe has bolstered its IP practice by recruiting a senior attorney from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO). The hire strengthens the firm's ability to handle complex patent issues as Orrick expands in technology and telecommunications sectors.The new attorney will join on the 28th of April, 2026, bringing extensive experience from the USPTO in patent examination and litigation, aligning with Orrick's aim to deliver comprehensive legal solutions for clients in fast-moving industries.Orrick's managing partner framed the hire as a signal of the firm's commitment to top-tier IP services and a deep understanding of patent law. The move fits within a broader strategy to adapt to the evolving landscape of intellectual property rights in the digital era.

+ ORRICK WELCOMES NICOLE LOPEZ, A LEADER IN YOUTH ONLINE SAFETY POLICY AND LITIGATION

Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the addition of Nicole Lopez to its team, naming her a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation. Lopez previously held a senior role at Meta and will help bolster the firm's capabilities as online safety challenges evolve. On Apr. 28, 2026, she expressed enthusiasm for the new role and the opportunity to broaden her impact.Lopez's background spans policy, litigation and regulatory strategy, which Orrick says will benefit clients facing heightened scrutiny over online safety. "I am so immensely proud of the pioneering work we did at Meta as a team, where I worked with incredibly talented colleagues who cared so deeply about getting it right," she said. Jeremy Kudon, who leads Orrick's State Legislative practice, noted escalated legislative activity with 25 state legislatures considering measures to safeguard minors online. "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large."

+ ORRICK NAMES FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY CHIEF TO ITS ROSTER

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe announced the addition of the former Meta youth safety policy head to its team, strengthening the firm's capabilities in digital security and regulatory compliance.The hire brings a veteran policymaker with hands-on experience in drafting and implementing guidelines around online youth protection, privacy, and user safety.The move signals Orrick's proactive stance toward evolving technology law and its aim to provide clients with guidance on privacy, data protection, and digital safety.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJW-8BN3-S333-S070-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ ADDING TO GLOBAL TOP FOUR EMERGING COMPANIES & VENTURE CAPITAL TEAM, PILLSBURY WELCOMES PARTNER JOHN ROCKWELL IN SILICON VALLEY

Continuing the expansion of Pillsbury's global Emerging Companies & Venture Capital practice, the firm has welcomed another well-known partner to the team: John Rockwell in Silicon Valley. Rockwell, who comes to Pillsbury from WilmerHale, brings valuable cross-border experience in Latin America and even greater depth to a practice that was recently ranked by LSEG as among the four most active in the world for the representation of company-side venture capital financings.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY PREVAILS IN WINERY DISPUTE AFTER FIVE-DAY JURY TRIAL

Ateam of Pillsbury lawyers led by Austin-based Litigation partner Casey Low prevailed in a five-day jury trial in favor of client Fred Schrader, the founder of the highly acclaimed Schrader Cellars winery, and the winery's current owner.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ KING & SPALDING STEPHEN VAUGHN NAMED ONE OF D.C.S 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE BY WASHINGTONIAN MAGAZINE

King &Spalding Partner Stephen Vaughn has been named one of Washington, D.C.'s 500 Most Influential People by Washingtonian magazine.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [5 Press Releases]

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADDS SEASONED FINANCE AND RESTRUCTURING SHAREHOLDER GERARD C. MARTIN IN CHICAGO

CHICAGO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has added secured finance attorney Gerard C. Martin to its Chicago office as a shareholder in the firm's Restructuring & Special Situations Practice. He joins the firm from Reed Smith LLP.

Martin brings deep experience across the full spectrum of financing transactions and debt restructurings. He represents lenders, borrowers, sponsors, and investors in a broad range of U.S. and international financing transactions, including cash flow and asset-based loans, leveraged loans, acquisition financings, bridge facilities, recapitalizations, and complex intercreditor arrangements, including unitranche and multitranche structures. He regularly advises on large, syndicated credit facilities and cross-border and multicurrency financings. Martin also maintains an active equipment and aviation finance practice, representing parties in aircraft and engine financings, leveraged leases, rolling stock transactions, and project finance matters. He works with clients across a wide range of industries, including manufacturing, energy, aviation, technology, health care, and financial services, among others.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISED REX CONCEPTS AND MCWIN ON THE INITIAL PUBLIC OFFERING AND LISTING ON THE WARSAW STOCK EXCHANGE

WARSAW -7 May 2026- Greenberg Traurig advised Rex Concepts S.A. (the "Company"), the master franchisee of Burger King and Popeyes in Poland, Czechia and Romania, as well as Rex Invest CEE - a company in the McWin group - as the selling shareholder, on the initial public offering (IPO) of the Company's shares and the admission of the shares to trading on the regulated market operated by the Warsaw Stock Exchange.

The total value of the offering amounted to approximately PLN 497.8 million, with a final offer price set at PLN 14 per share. + GREENBERG TRAURIG ADVISES SELLER ON HYPERSCALE DATA CENTER TRANSACTION IN JAPAN

TOKYO - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented the seller in the disposition of a stabilized hyperscale data center located in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. The transaction is among the largest asset‑level sales of a fully leased, operational hyperscale data center in the Japanese market to date.

The approximately 40‑megawatt facility is leased to a single U.S. hyperscale cloud provider. The seller is a joint venture between a fund sponsored by a U.S. institutional manager and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company, and the buyer is a consortium comprising Singapore‑listed CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, which acquired a 49% interest, and a fund managed by an affiliate of the Japanese trading company, which acquired the remaining 51%.

NEW YORK - May 7, 2026 - Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has strengthened its market-leading Real Estate and Hospitality practices with the addition of two seasoned cross-border attorneys focused on hospitality, real estate, and international matters. Carlos A. Sugich joins as co-chair of the Americas Hospitality Group in Phoenix and Mexico, and Carlos Freaner joins as a shareholder in Mexico. They arrive from Snell & Wilmer.

He led the launch of his prior firm's Los Cabos office nearly two decades ago.

urn:contentItem:6JK1-S443-S3V9-01PX-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:03:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - UPDATES FROM PERKINS COIE AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [1 News That Matters]

+ MORGAN LEWIS TAPS PILLSBURY TALENT TO DEEPEN BENEFITS PRACTICE

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has added a prominent Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP attorney to its Employee Benefits team, reflecting a strategic response to rising complexity in benefits law.The attorney's background encompasses compliance and regulatory issues across employee benefits, with expectations to strengthen the firm's ability to provide comprehensive legal services.Arriving on 08th April 2026, the hire supports Morgan Lewis' ongoing plan to expand its benefits practice in a changing regulatory environment, while Pillsbury has seen departures to other firms.The move underscores the competitive talent market in corporate law and signals Morgan Lewis' commitment to maintaining state-of-the-art capabilities for its diverse client base.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST AND PERKINS COIE LAUNCH GLOBAL FIRM ASHURST PERKINS COIE TO EXPAND CROSS-BORDER OFFERINGS

On 13th April 2026 the partnership vote to merge Ashurst and Perkins Coie into Ashurst Perkins Coie was approved, with a closing target in the third quarter subject to customary conditions.The leadership structure will feature Bill Malley and Paul Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Karen Davies of Ashurst and Brian Eiting of Perkins Coie will serve as co-chairs.The merged group will comprise about 3,000 lawyers in 52 offices across 32 countries, with core hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney and New York and a focus on technology, energy, infrastructure and financial services.The combined revenues are around USD 2.8 billion, reflecting extensive cross-border experience across cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore and Tokyo. The aim is to sharpen competitive edge and position the firm for ongoing growth in the global market.

+ GLOBAL LAW FIRMS PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE UNDER ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination, reflecting strong support for a union that combines geographic reach with sector depth and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will become a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion and about 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. The combined firm will be noted for its technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services capabilities, aligned with the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked that the partnership's approval signals a belief that the merger will deliver a differentiated global platform with scale and leadership in technology to meet cross-border client needs. 'Our partnership's approval reflects a belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform.'Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed the sentiment, emphasizing alignment between the firms and their joint future ambitions. 'This vote confirms our strong alignment and the compelling platform it creates for clients and talent alike,' he said. The parties noted that the merger will enhance cross-border service capability across key sectors.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET THROUGH SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has opened a Shenzhen representative office, signaling a renewed foothold in China. The office was inaugurated on Apr. 14, 2026. The move follows the 2024 closure of the firm's Beijing and Shanghai offices and reflects a strategic pivot toward Shenzhen to leverage the city's growing tech and manufacturing base in the Greater Bay Area. Bing Ai and Wei Yuan will lead the office as chief representative and managing partner and as representative and litigation partner, respectively; both are US-trained and bring deep experience advising technology clients. Ai focuses on patent procurement and portfolio management for semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan handles patent infringement disputes. Bill Malley, Perkins Coie's managing partner, says the Shenzhen launch reinforces the firm's cross-border US legal capabilities and its aim to help clients navigate global markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY GRABS $60 MILLION SERIES B BACKING

Crosby has raised $60 million in a Series B, a mark of its rapid fundraising that was announced on April 3, 2026.The round was led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from Sequoia, 01 Advisors, Bain Capital Ventures and angel investor Elad Gil, with Cooley LLP listed among the backers.Funds will be used to expand Crosby’s technology and support a growing client base that includes Clay, Rogo and Ramp.The company blends artificial intelligence with legal know-how to speed contract reviews and says the total value of its negotiated contracts now exceeds $1 billion, up from about $30 million when it emerged from stealth last year. In a blog post, Crosby wrote, "As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences." The firm has introduced simulations of counterparty responses to redlines, voice agents that negotiate for clients, and a dashboard for clients to oversee Crosby’s work. Crosby was founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, with Daniels a former Cooley associate and Sarihan a former Google software engineer and Ramp tech lead.

+ COOLEY WELCOMES FRESHFIELDS VETERANS TO BOLSTER TECHNOLOGY GROUP

Cooley LLP announced a substantial extension of its technology practice through the addition of several attorneys formerly with Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. The move is designed to enhance Cooley's tech capabilities and underscores the firm's aim to remain competitive amid a dynamic legal environment.As of 23rd November 2025, the recruitment drive underscores the firm's continued push to expand technology offerings in a rapidly evolving sector. The infusion of Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer alumni is expected to strengthen the existing practice and draw new clients needing technology-focused legal counsel.The incoming team members come with extensive experience advising leading clients on IP, data protection, and corporate tech transactions. Their expertise is expected to augment Cooley's well-established venture capital and startup advisory services, further cementing the firm’s leadership in technology law.Cooley's decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader market trend in the legal industry: firms actively pursue top talent to meet demands of a fast-moving, tech-centric market. As tech-related legal challenges grow more complex, the need for specialized knowledge becomes critical, making these hires timely.With this growth, Cooley aims to solidify its status as a preferred destination for technology clients, reinforcing its capabilities in an area essential for innovation and growth. As integration proceeds, observers will monitor the impact on Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH BOLSTERS PRIVATE EQUITY PRACTICE IN LOS ANGELES WITH NEW PARTNER

Reed Smith has announced the addition of a private equity partner in its Los Angeles office, strengthening its capability in a market that remains pivotal for private capital flows.The new partner joins on 4th May 2026 and brings deep experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services, aligning with the firm's plan to broaden its private equity practice and serve investment firms and corporate clients involved in M&A.The appointment underscores Reed Smith's aim to provide strategic insights and tailored solutions as the private equity landscape evolves, supporting sophisticated investment structures and regulatory considerations.This strategic hire positions Reed Smith to pursue growth opportunities in private equity and continue delivering high-quality service and durable client relationships.

+ REED SMITH ANNOUNCES PHILADELPHIA OFFICE LEADER FROM WITHIN

ReedSmith LLP has tapped an internal attorney to lead its Philadelphia office, signaling a strategic shift in a key market. The new leader has more than a decade with the firm and has contributed to notable cases and regional growth, with focus on client relationships and innovative solutions. Effective from 27th April 2026, the appointment reinforces Reed Smith's plan to bolster its local presence and maintain continuity across leadership, while expanding capabilities in the Philadelphia market.

+ REED SMITH ESTABLISHES FIRST BOSTON MANAGING PARTNER POSITION TO DEEPEN MARKET PRESENCE

ReedSmith LLP announced the appointment of its first Managing Partner in Boston, a move designed to intensify the firm's regional focus. The appointment is effective from 23rd April 2026. The Boston leadership will oversee local operations, with a mandate to deepen client relationships and drive growth across sectors in the Northeast. The firm said the decision reflects the region's rising demand for specialized legal services and its readiness to reinforce its presence with a dedicated leadership role. With extensive experience and a broad network, the new Managing Partner is expected to accelerate operational efficiency and strengthen Reed Smith's reputation in Boston, positioning the office as a key driver of the firm's long-term strategy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ SPACE BIOTECHNOLOGY STARTUP BIOORBIT CLOSES $13.2M SEED ROUND; WSGR HELPS GUIDE FINANCING

BioOrbit has announced a seed round of $13.2 million, co-led by LocalGlobe and BREEGA, with contributions from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, 7percent Ventures and others. The financing was announced on 30th April 2026.BioOrbit aims to establish a space-based pharmaceuticals factory within the next decade and is developing hardware to enable large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, a core step in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.The company is led by Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, who are spearheading this space-focused platform.Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. provided corporate governance and emerging-company counsel to BioOrbit during the financing, with the team including Daniel Glazer, Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed and Sophia Kunthara.The deal underscores rising investor interest in space-enabled life sciences, and BioOrbit will share updates on LinkedIn and in UKTN coverage as plans progress.

+ WILSON SONSINI SUPPORTS OFF PISTE CAPITAL IN LANDMARK FUND DEBUT BACKED BY EDITION VENTURES

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. advised Off Piste Capital in forming its inaugural fund, Off Piste Fund I, which closed on 29th April 2026 with backing from strategic limited partners including Edition Ventures.The fund targets investments in companies building the systems and infrastructure of the future. Off Piste has already invested in four startups: Stendr, a Norway-based firm delivering AI-driven counter-drone technology; Objection, a UK startup creating accountability infrastructure for public information; Beyond Reach Labs, a US YC-backed venture developing deployable space structures; and Octet, a UK company specializing in cryptographic proofs of physical location.The deal team at Wilson Sonsini included Bradley Doline, Alice Cao, and Stephanie Yasay, whose work on capital markets and fund formation helped navigate the complexities of launching a VC fund. This collaboration underscores the firm's ongoing support for emerging venture activity and its expanding footprint in the tech investment landscape.

+ WILSON SONSINI ELEVATES ENERGY TRANSITION CAPABILITIES WITH KEN SANOCKI APPOINTMENT

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati has added Ken Sanocki as a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, aimed at expanding the firm's ability to help clients finance energy infrastructure through the transition to cleaner power.Sanocki comes from Sunrun, where as vice president of legal he oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and complex debt structures for distributed-energy projects. He led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion.Doug Clark, the firm's managing partner, said that Sanocki has operated at the center of the energy transition and will bring deep knowledge of tax equity and structured financings to clients navigating energy financing.In his new role, Sanocki will counsel on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, assist with capital formation and strategic transactions, and help clients develop revenue-generating asset portfolios and prepare for IPOs or M&A. He began his career at Milbank after serving as SunEdison senior counsel for global structured and project finance; he earned a J.D. from NYU and a B.A. from Boston College.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN GUIDES TELIX IN 50:50 CO-DEVELOPMENT AND CO-COMMERCIALISATION DEAL WITH REGENERON WORTH USD 2.1 BILLION

Telix Pharmaceuticals has enlisted Sidley Austin to guide its collaboration with Regeneron, a deal valued at up to USD 2.1 billion that covers the co-development and co-commercialisation of next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. The arrangement provides for a 50:50 split of costs and profits and includes an upfront USD 40 million payment for four initial programmes. Announced on 21st April 2026, the agreement is built to pool the companies' strengths in oncology and radiopharmaceuticals, aiming to move candidates through development more efficiently while sharing regulatory and governance responsibilities. Sidley partner Joshua Hofheimer noted that governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property are central to these collaborations, stating that "These considerations affect governance, regulatory matters, and intellectual property, in particular." The alliance reflects a broader push in the biopharma space toward strategic collaborations designed to accelerate innovation in cancer care, a trend that law firms like Sidley are well-placed to support given the increasing complexity of cross-border deals.

+ RECRUITMENT PUSH DRIVES 33% GROWTH IN SIDLEY AUSTIN'S CITY OPERATIONS

Sidley Austin reported a 33% rise in City revenue, attributed to its hiring strategy as of 14th April 2026. Management credits a focused approach to identifying and integrating skilled professionals that expanded the City teams and diversified practice areas. The improvements in service have helped lift billable hours and client retention, reinforcing confidence in the hiring model. Analysts say the move demonstrates a willingness to invest in human capital to keep pace with a shifting legal market.

+ REGULATOR ALIGNS WITH TOP FIRMS AS CFTC CREATES INNOVATION TASK FORCE

The CFTC has formed an Innovation Task Force with Sidley Austin and Latham & Watkins to strengthen the regulatory framework for derivatives markets as technology reshapes the sector.The 11th April 2026 announcement notes the collaboration aims to incorporate insights from both firms to reflect evolving market realities, including digital assets and new trading technologies.Sidley Austin will provide regulatory guidance while Latham & Watkins contributes practical policy experience, helping the commission evaluate how tools like blockchain and AI intersect with oversight objectives.The task force is part of a wider effort to balance prudent supervision with fostering innovation, as the U.S. seeks to maintain leadership in global financial markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDEN ENTERTAINMENT BECOMES PRIVATE, FAMILY-OWNED IN TRANSACTION WITH GREENBERG TRAURIG

Greenberg Traurig advised Blake L. Sartini in moving Golden Entertainment to private family ownership, with the closing on 30th April 2026.Under the terms, Golden Entertainment shareholders received a cash dividend of $2.75 and 0.902 shares of VICI Properties Inc. for each share of Golden stock, providing liquidity while keeping an equity stake in the gaming and hospitality company.VICI Properties bought the real estate assets of seven Golden casino resorts, while a triple-net master lease was arranged with Sartini-controlled entities, enabling Golden to continue operating its full portfolio without disruption.Michael J. Bonner, Co-Managing Shareholder of Greenberg Traurig's Las Vegas office, remarked, "We were honored to represent Blake L. Sartini in returning Golden Entertainment to private family ownership. We congratulate Blake and the entire Golden Entertainment team!" The firm also highlighted its expertise across corporate transactions and real estate law, with a team including Dmitriy A. Tartakovskiy, Jim Mace, and James Doyle.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG IN TOKYO CHARTS AI-DRIVEN SHIFTS IN GLOBAL REAL ASSET MARKETS

Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office hosted a briefing for ANREV on Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities on the 30th of April, 2026. Panelist Joel H. Rothstein, who chairs the Asia Real Estate Practice, joined colleagues to discuss AI's effects on capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies, with emphasis on growing data centre activity in the Asia-Pacific amid digitalization. Rothstein stressed the firm's commitment to industry dialogue and noted that the conversations reflect the convergence of digital infrastructure, real estate, and emerging technologies. The team draws on Greenberg Traurig's global platform to support cross-border investments in real assets. Greenberg Traurig operates about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations and has been recognized for its real estate and infrastructure work, including a BTI Best of the Best honor in 2025 and listings on Am Law Global 100 and Law360 400.

+ LAW ROCKS LA: GREENBERG TRAURIG BAND THE MELTONES CLINCH TOP HONOR

On 29th April 2026, The Meltones, Greenberg Traurig, LLP’s house band, took top honors at Law Rocks Los Angeles, held at Whisky A Go Go in West Hollywood. The event raised more than $65,000 in donations and sponsorships, including $41,000 for The Headstrong Project.Over three nights, Law Rocks Los Angeles set a new fundraising benchmark with a total that surpassed $569,000, the highest in 15 years for the city. The Meltones opened with a Green Day cover, and victory was determined by audience support, ticket sales, and judges’ scores. GT was a Hall of Fame sponsor.The Meltones feature a cross-section of GT talent, with notable members such as Stephen L. Rabinowitz and Burt Bruton on guitar and Christopher A. Cernik on keyboard; vocalists include Michael R. Hogue, Ashley A. LeBlanc, and Tracy Newirth, with Tom Helf on drums and Daniel Blumberg on bass joining as guests. The band carries the legacy of co-founder Mel Greenberg and remains a core part of GT’s philanthropic ethos, which also includes a recent Law Rocks NYC win raising over $100,000 for The Door; the firm now employs about 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESPERION'S $1.1B PRIVATISATION SIGNALS PRIVATE-EQUITY PULL IN BIOTECH, UNDER LEGAL HELM OF SIDLEY AND GOODWIN

Esperion Therapeutics Inc. has agreed to a $1.1 billion take-private, with Sidley Austin LLP and Goodwin Procter LLP leading the legal work. The announcement, made on 1st May 2026, positions the lipid-management specialist to focus on its programs outside the public markets.Private ownership is gaining traction in biotech as firms seek to streamline R&D and accelerate commercialization.Sidley and Goodwin's involvement reflects their strength in complex M&A deals and signals rigorous governance as Esperion transitions to a private structure.Analysts note that the move could enable longer-term strategic planning and potentially faster growth, while investors monitor the company's performance in its new privately held state.

+ FBT GIBBONS TAPS GOODWIN PROCTER VETERAN TO STRENGTHEN VC CAPABILITIES

FBT Gibbons is expanding its capabilities in venture capital by hiring from Goodwin Procter LLP for its New Jersey office. On 21st April 2026, the firm confirmed the recruitment of a venture capital attorney. The attorney comes with extensive venture capital experience from Goodwin Procter LLP, a leader in the field, and the firm plans to leverage that expertise for clients seeking guidance on capital raises. Industry observers note the hire reflects a broader trend of law firms sharpening niche practices to compete in a growing market.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER ENABLES CLOSURE OF $328 MILLION CONSUMER-FOCUSED FUND FOR TOPSPIN

Goodwin Procter LLP helped close Topspin Consumer Partners' latest fundraising, securing a $328 million commitment for a consumer-focused fund. The deal underscores a growing trend in private equity toward consumer-oriented strategies, with Goodwin Procter providing crucial legal support throughout the process.On April 15, 2026, Topspin announced the fund’s closure, designed to pursue opportunities in the consumer sector. The firm’s counsel was instrumental in navigating fundraising requirements, maintaining regulatory compliance, and simplifying investor workflows.The fund intends to target a broad spectrum of opportunities across consumer industries, emphasizing segments with strong growth trajectories. With Goodwin Procter’s guidance, Topspin Consumer Partners aims to back innovative ventures well positioned to compete in a dynamic market.This partnership reinforces Goodwin Procter’s leadership in private equity work and its dedication to client success, with potential implications for investor confidence as the fund progresses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ ORRICK WELCOMES SENIOR USPTO LAWYER AS IP PRACTICE GROWS

Orrick has expanded its IP practice by hiring a senior attorney from the USPTO, aiming to enhance capabilities in patent matters as the firm grows in tech and telecom.The new attorney will join on the 28th of April, 2026, and brings extensive patent examination and litigation experience.Orrick's leadership described the hire as evidence of the firm's commitment to top-tier IP services and to navigating the evolving IP rights landscape in the digital age.With demand for technology-focused counsel rising, the firm expects this addition to support clients facing complex patent challenges and strengthen its competitive standing.

+ ORRICK EXPANDS ONLINE SAFETY CAPABILITIES WITH NICOLE LOPEZ

Orrick has added Nicole Lopez, a leading authority in youth online safety policy and litigation, to its team. Lopez previously held a significant position at Meta, and on Apr. 28, 2026, she expressed enthusiasm for the new role.Her hiring comes as 25 state legislatures weigh measures to safeguard minors online, a shift Kudon calls a watershed moment. "We are facing a watershed moment. Online safety is top of mind for innovators, government, teens and parents, and society at large," Kudon said.Aravind Swaminathan praised Lopez as a multifaceted asset—"part policy expert, part litigation and regulatory strategist, and part business advisor"—and highlighted the Online Safety Law Center as a hub for guiding clients through rapid changes in the digital space.

+ FORMER META YOUTH SAFETY POLICY LEAD JOINS ORRICK

On Apr. 23, 2026, Orrick announced the addition of the former Meta youth safety policy head to its team, a move designed to expand its digital safety and compliance capabilities.The new hire comes with a strong track record in policy design and implementation, particularly around privacy and the protection of young users online.Orrick frames the appointment as part of its proactive response to evolving regulatory requirements governing youth online safety.The firm expects the hire to strengthen its advisory services as tech companies face heightened scrutiny and regulatory complexity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
